- **Dual-state first card:** The top card doubles as a "currently active" persistent mini-player with contextual controls (unmute/join), not just another feed item. This pattern avoids a separate sticky player UI while still surfacing the active session.
- **FAB placement mid-scroll:** The FAB floats over content rather than being pinned to a corner — creates a more centered, approachable feel and draws attention without a fixed overlay layer.
- **Emoji as visual texture:** Room titles and club names use emoji inline with text to add personality and scannability without custom iconography — low-cost expressiveness.
- **Ghost + filled button pairing:** Two actions (unmute / join) use opposite button weights to establish clear hierarchy without color differentiation — a clean way to present primary vs. secondary in tight space.
- **Club name as metadata label:** Small icon + club name above the room title creates a consistent "source label" pattern — helps users understand context/community before reading the room title.

---

## Notes
- Scatter avatars instead of rows → feels alive, not tabular
- Cream cards on white screen = subtle depth without shadow
- Blue used ONLY for action — never decorative, very disciplined
- Emoji in titles = free personality layer, no design cost
- Active card ≠ sticky player — embed state into feed item itself
- Ghost + filled side-by-side = hierarchy without color change
- FAB centered in scroll feels inviting vs. corner feels utilitarian
- "+N" badge = social proof signal, keep it understated
- Club icon as tiny avatar = source credibility at a glance

## Screen
On-demand grocery/delivery app home screen. Entry point of the main app flow after authentication. Vertical scrolling feed layout with a sticky header containing location selector, utility icons, and a global search bar. Below: category shortcuts, promotional banner, store grid, cause-marketing card, and a deals section beginning to peek in.

---

## Layout & Spacing

Content is organized in distinct horizontal bands/sections with clear vertical rhythm. Category icons sit in a tight 4-column row. The store grid uses a 4-column layout with two rows visible plus a "Show all" tile. Sections are separated by generous whitespace (~20–24px gaps) rather than dividers. The promotional credit banner uses full-width card treatment to break the grid rhythm and demand attention. Horizontal scroll is implied for the cause-marketing cards at the bottom (partial card peek visible on right edge).

---

## Components

- **Location selector**: Text + chevron dropdown — minimal, left-aligned in header
- **Search bar**: Full-width pill shape, light gray fill, magnifier icon prefix, placeholder copy spans all verticals ("products, stores, and restaurants")
- **Category icon tiles**: Circular white background with illustrated emoji-style icons, label below, "New" badge on one (red pill, white text)
- **Promo/credit banner**: Rounded card, white bg, dollar amount in large bold, supporting copy in smaller weight, pill CTA button right-aligned
- **Store tiles**: Square rounded cards, white bg, logo-centered; delivery time shown in small gray text below name; discount badges in yellow pill (e.g., "$25 off", "$15 off"); "In-store prices" badge in teal/green pill
- **"Show all" tile**: Dark circular icon with arrow — acts as navigation affordance within the grid
- **Cause-marketing card**: Full-bleed dark green card with map texture overlay, white headline, pill CTA button in white/outline style, heart icon top-left
- **Bottom nav**: 4 tabs with icons + labels; "New" badge on Restaurants tab mirrors category badge

---

## Typography & Color

**Type hierarchy:**
- Location: medium weight, ~16px
- Search placeholder: light/regular, ~15px
- Promo dollar amount: heavy/bold, ~28px — largest text on screen
- Section headers ("Score touchdown deals"): bold, ~20px
- Store names: medium, ~13px
- Supporting copy: regular/light, ~12px gray

**Color palette:**
- Background: off-white/light gray
- Cards: white
- Accent badges: yellow (#F5C518-ish) for discounts, teal/green for "In-store prices," red for "New" badge
- Cause card: deep forest green with subtle map texture
- CTAs: dark pill buttons (near-black) and white outline pill on dark bg
- Functional color is used tightly — yellow = savings, green = price parity, red = novelty

---

## Patterns

- **Unified search scope**: Single search bar explicitly covers all verticals (products, stores, restaurants) — reduces cognitive load of choosing a category before searching
- **Delivery time as trust signal**: Each store tile shows "By [time]" — anchors urgency and sets expectations before tapping in
- **Badge taxonomy**: Three distinct badge types (discount %, price type, novelty) each with unique color coding — creates scannable at-a-glance store comparison
- **"Show all" as grid tile**: Rather than a text link below the grid, the "show all 121 stores" is embedded as a final tile in the grid itself — keeps eyes in the grid, reduces layout interruption
- **Cause card as content, not ad**: Donation CTA is styled like editorial content (map texture, bold headline) rather than a banner ad — increases engagement likelihood
- **Partial card peek**: Right edge of a second card visible in the cause-marketing row signals horizontal scrollability without any explicit indicator
- **Credit nudge placement**: Unused credits card placed immediately below category nav — high-visibility placement for a retention/re-engagement mechanic
- **"New" badge mirroring**: The "New" badge appears on both the category icon row AND the bottom nav tab for Restaurants — reinforces the signal across two navigation surfaces simultaneously

---

## Notes

- Badge color system = instant store comparison layer — steal this
- Delivery time as subtitle on every tile is underrated UX — sets expectation before commitment
- "Show all" as grid tile > text link — keeps spatial flow intact
- Search placeholder copy that spans categories = implicit IA education
- Cause card texture treatment elevates it from banner blindness
- Credit nudge right after nav = smart re-engagement without modal interruption
- Dual "New" badge placement (icon + nav) — reinforcement without repetition feeling

## Screen
Community/messaging app server channel list. This is the main navigation hub for a large community server — sits between the server selection rail and individual channel views. Layout is a vertical stack: hero banner → server identity header → utility actions → flat channel list with category groupings. Primary interactive elements: search bar, icon action buttons (add member, calendar), collapsible category headers, individual channel rows.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Content flows top-to-bottom in clear zones: full-bleed banner image (~140px), identity block with name/meta, action row, then scrollable channel list. Channel rows have consistent left-padding with icon + emoji + label alignment. Category headers use reduced font weight and a collapse chevron as visual separators without heavy dividers. Left sidebar rail (server switcher) overlaps the main panel slightly, creating a layered depth effect. Generous vertical rhythm between channel rows — not cramped despite density.

---

## Components
- **Hero banner**: Full-width 3D illustrated image, no overlay text — purely decorative/brand
- **Server identity block**: Bold name + emoji + verified badge + chevron (expandable info) + member count + type tag in muted text
- **Search bar**: Pill-shaped, light fill, leading search icon, centered placeholder — lower visual weight than a typical input
- **Icon buttons**: Two square rounded-rect buttons (add member, calendar) alongside search — consistent size, outlined icon style
- **Channel rows**: Icon glyph (channel type) + emoji + text label. No background, tap-target full width
- **Category headers**: Small caps-ish label with chevron — collapsible section dividers
- **NEW badge**: Solid purple pill label — high contrast attention marker
- **Left rail**: Circular avatar thumbnails with notification dot overlay; add (+) and hierarchy icons below

---

## Typography & Color
- Server name: ~18px bold, dark near-black
- Member count/type: ~13px, muted gray — clear secondary hierarchy
- Channel names: ~15px regular, medium gray — readable but not competing
- Category labels: ~12px, lighter gray, uppercase-adjacent weight
- Background: Clean white for list, very light gray for overall app chrome
- Accent: Purple used exclusively for the NEW badge and verified checkmark — color reserved for status/novelty signals
- Emoji used as inline color — all other UI is achromatic, letting emoji carry visual warmth

---

## Patterns
- **Emoji as wayfinding**: Channel names lead with emoji instead of colored icons — community-sourced personality without custom iconography overhead. Scales infinitely, zero design maintenance
- **3D illustration banner with no text overlay**: Decorative header sets tone/brand without competing with functional content below — clean separation of identity vs. utility
- **Achromatic UI + emoji color strategy**: Entire chrome is grayscale; all color expression delegated to user-generated emoji. Creates consistency while allowing infinite community customization
- **Verified + chevron on server name**: Packs trust signal, identity, and "more info" affordance into one compact line
- **NEW badge scarcity**: Only one item gets the badge — makes it feel meaningful rather than noisy
- **Category collapse as progressive disclosure**: Long channel lists become manageable; headers double as navigation landmarks
- **Search bar width compression**: Search doesn't span full width — leaves room for contextual action buttons, treating the row as a toolbar rather than a search-first experience
- **Sidebar rail layering**: Server switcher partially bleeds into view, reinforcing spatial navigation model (you are inside a server, others exist to the left)

---

## Notes
- Emoji-as-icon system = zero design debt, infinite personality — steal this for any user-generated community product
- Achromatic base + user color = scalable theming without brand collision
- 3D hero art with no text = brand moment that doesn't fight utility
- Badge scarcity rule: only badge truly new/changed items, never more than 1-2 at a time
- Search-as-toolbar pattern: compress search width, add contextual actions alongside — better than full-width search dominating the row
- Category headers as collapsible landmarks — good for any long flat list that needs scannable grouping without heavy visual dividers
- Verified badge + expandable chevron on entity name = trust + discovery in one line

## Screen
Card management screen within a fintech/banking app. This sits in the account management flow, accessible via bottom nav. Layout is a single-column vertical stack: page title + utility icons → card visual → quick actions → wallet integration CTA → physical card activation banner → bottom nav.

## Layout & Spacing
Content flows in clearly separated zones with generous whitespace between each section. The card visual takes up roughly 40% of the viewport width-wise with comfortable horizontal padding. Quick action buttons are centered as a trio below the card, creating a visual "control panel" feel. The wallet CTA and activation banner are full-width, creating clear visual weight hierarchy. Sections feel chunked rather than continuous — each block has its own breathing room.

## Components
- **Card visual**: White card with subtle rounded corners, light inner glow/glare effect (diagonal highlight), payment network logo bottom-right. Clean, premium feel.
- **Quick action buttons**: Three circular icon buttons with light gray fill, icon + label below. Uniform sizing, evenly spaced.
- **Wallet CTA button**: Full-width pill/rounded rectangle, near-black fill, white text, wallet emoji/icon left-aligned inside button. High contrast, dominant.
- **Activation banner**: Warm cream/off-white card with bold headline, supporting text, inline CTA button (black, rounded), and a decorative stacked card illustration floating right.
- **Top utility row**: Help (circle with ?) and overflow (···) icons, both in light gray pill/circle containers.

## Typography & Color
- **Type hierarchy**: Bold large title ("Card") → bold section headline ("Activate physical card") → regular body ("Expected arrival…") → small labels under icons
- **Weights**: Heavy for titles, medium for labels, regular for supporting copy
- **Color palette**: Near-white background (#F2F2F2 range), black for primary actions and text, warm cream for the activation card, gold/tan for the brand wordmark, payment network red/orange circles
- **Color function**: Black = primary action, cream = secondary/pending state, gray = tertiary/utility actions

## Patterns
- **Dual-state card onboarding**: The screen simultaneously handles digital card (already active, can add to wallet) and physical card (pending, needs activation) — two distinct states surfaced in one view without confusion. Each has its own visual treatment and CTA.
- **Glare/reflection on card**: Subtle diagonal light sweep on the card visual adds physicality and premium feel without being skeuomorphic.
- **Contextual urgency without alarm**: "Expected arrival 1–15 Nov" sets expectation passively — no red, no badge, no push — just calm informational framing with an action available when ready.
- **Icon action trio as secondary nav**: Freeze/View/Settings acts as a mini control panel specific to the card object — a pattern borrowed from object-level toolbars, not page-level navigation.
- **Decorative illustration in banner**: The stacked card illustration in the activation banner adds personality and reinforces the physical card concept without using a photo — keeps the design system consistent.
- **Help icon proximity to title**: Placing the help icon adjacent to the page title suggests contextual help tied to this specific screen, not global support.

## Notes
- Cream banner = great "pending state" visual language — warm, non-urgent, distinct from main UI
- Card glare effect = cheap way to add premium feel, worth replicating
- Freeze icon (snowflake) = universally understood for card lock — smart icon choice
- Two CTAs (digital + physical) coexist without competing — sequencing via visual weight (black button first, softer banner second)
- Floating illustration in banner breaks grid slightly — adds life
- Consider this pattern for any "object management" screen: card/asset visual → quick actions → integrations → status banners

## Screen
Health/nutrition tracking app — daily diary dashboard. This is the primary home screen users land on after onboarding. Layout splits vertically: full-bleed gradient hero zone occupying roughly the top 45%, transitioning into a white card-based content area below. Primary interactive elements: calorie ring, "See Stats" expand trigger, macro progress bars, date navigator, day rating card, and a centered FAB in the nav bar.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Hero section uses edge-to-edge color with no card containment — data floats directly on the gradient. The three-column calorie layout (eaten / ring / burned) uses the center ring as a visual anchor, with flanking numbers creating symmetry. Below the fold, content shifts to white rounded cards with generous internal padding (~20px). The macro card and day rating card feel like separate "modules" rather than one continuous list. Date navigator sits between cards as a standalone row, acting as a section divider.

---

## Components
- **Calorie ring**: Large circular progress indicator, white stroke only, no fill arc visible — functions more as a framing device than a traditional progress ring. Number inside is the primary focal point.
- **Macro bars**: Three-column layout, each with a colored pill-shaped progress bar (orange = carbs, purple/pink = protein, indigo = fat). Bars are short and thick, not thin lines.
- **Date navigator**: Minimal — chevrons flanking a calendar icon + date label. Clean, low-visual-weight.
- **Day rating card**: Rounded card with emoji avatar (yellow smiley), large serif-style heading, body copy, hairline divider, and a green text CTA. Feels editorial.
- **FAB**: Green circle with plus, centered in tab bar — elevated above the bar visually.
- **Pagination dots**: Top-right corner, suggesting onboarding or swipeable intro context (this is a demo/showcase frame).

---

## Typography & Color
**Type hierarchy**: Large numerals (calorie data) dominate the hero — likely 48–56px, light weight, white. Small caps labels ("EATEN," "BURNED," "KCAL OVER") at ~10px tracking-heavy. Below fold: serif or high-contrast sans for "Day rating" heading (~28px), regular weight body copy at ~14px. "SEE MORE" in small caps green matches brand accent.

**Color**: Hero uses a soft, painterly gradient — green, purple, peach, coral — desaturated enough to feel premium rather than garish. All white text on gradient. Below fold is neutral white (#fff or near). Accent green used only for active states (diary tab, FAB, CTA links). Macro bar colors are categorical: orange/purple/indigo — distinct but harmonious.

---

## Patterns
- **Gradient as emotional tone-setter**: The hero gradient isn't decorative — it communicates the app's personality (soft, wellness-forward, non-clinical) before any data is read. The gradient shifts mood without requiring illustration.
- **Ring as frame, not meter**: The calorie ring doesn't show fill progress — it's a visual container that groups the deficit number. This avoids showing failure state prominently while still surfacing the number.
- **Deficit framing**: "273 KCAL OVER" is surfaced centrally but without alarm — no red, no warning iconography. Neutral white treatment normalizes the overage rather than punishing it.
- **Macro bars as status glyphs**: The thick, short bars communicate over/under at a glance via bar fill ratio without requiring the user to parse numbers first. Numbers are secondary confirmation.
- **Day rating as gamification hook**: Converting nutritional compliance into a "day rating" with an emoji avatar reframes health data as a score — more motivating than raw numbers. The emoji state (neutral smiley) implies room for improvement without negativity.
- **"See Stats" progressive disclosure**: Collapsible stats section keeps the hero clean while signaling there's more depth available — respects both casual and power users.
- **Contextual coaching copy**: "Eat more healthy carbs to boost your day rating" — specific, actionable, tied to the visible metric. Not generic encouragement.

---

## Notes
- Gradient hero = brand personality delivery mechanism, not decoration
- Ring without fill arc = clever way to show deficit without "failure" visual
- Emoji as health score avatar — gamification without game aesthetics
- Macro bars: thick pill shape > thin lines for glanceability
- White card zone as "work area" vs gradient as "status zone" — clear spatial roles
- Small caps labels throughout = consistent voice, premium feel
- FAB in tab bar center = always-accessible logging, never more than one tap away
- Coaching copy tied to specific metric = feels personalized, not boilerplate
- Date nav as section separator — functional + structural

## Screen
Mobile banking home screen — the primary dashboard of a personal finance app. Sits at the root of the navigation hierarchy. Two-zone layout: a colored hero header containing account balance and spending summary, transitioning into a white scrollable transaction list below.

## Layout & Spacing
The screen splits into a dark teal header (~40% of visible area) and a white content area below. The header uses generous vertical padding around the balance figure, making it feel spacious and premium. The spending summary card sits inset within the header with a subtle border, creating a card-within-header pattern. Transaction rows use consistent vertical rhythm with date headers acting as section dividers. The transition between header and content is a hard edge (no overlap or card lift), keeping zones clearly separated.

## Components
- **Account selector pill** — small rounded pill with label and chevron, low-contrast on dark background, feels secondary to balance
- **Balance display** — oversized bold numeral with a small `+` action button inline, right of the figure
- **Spending summary card** — semi-transparent bordered card with two columns (date range spend / today's spend), muted label text above values
- **Transaction date headers** — lightweight rows pairing date label (left) with daily net total (right)
- **Transaction rows** — icon avatar left, merchant name + time · category center, amount right; merchant names blurred for privacy
- **Transaction icons** — rounded square icons with illustrated/emoji-style graphics and currency label badge beneath
- **Search action** — text+icon inline link, blue, top-right of transactions section
- **Profile/menu icons** — top-right of header, avatar icon + hamburger, both on teal

## Typography & Color
**Type hierarchy:** Balance = largest, heaviest weight (display size, bold). Section labels ("Transactions") = medium bold. Date headers = regular weight, muted gray. Transaction merchant names = medium bold. Supporting metadata (time, category) = small, gray. Amounts = right-aligned, weight varies by sign.

**Color palette:**
- Deep teal gradient header (dark green to mid teal)
- Pure white content area
- Green (`+£` values) — income signaling
- Neutral dark for debit amounts — no red, intentionally calm
- Blue for interactive text (Search)
- Purple circle avatar for one merchant logo

Color is used functionally: green = money in, neutral = money out (avoids anxiety-inducing red), teal header = brand ownership of the most important number.

## Patterns
- **Inline balance action** — the `+` button sits directly beside the balance figure rather than in a separate action bar, reducing cognitive distance between "seeing money" and "adding money"
- **Account type selector as pill** — account switching is accessible but visually subordinate; doesn't compete with the balance
- **Daily net in date headers** — each date group header shows the net movement for that day on the right, giving a scannable financial summary without opening any transaction
- **No red for debits** — deliberate choice to show outgoing amounts in neutral dark rather than red, reducing financial anxiety while still being legible
- **Currency badge on transaction icon** — small EUR label beneath the icon communicates foreign currency at a glance without cluttering the row
- **Spending summary split** — "period spend" vs "today's spend" side-by-side creates instant temporal comparison in minimal space
- **Blurred merchant names** — privacy-first default in screenshot/demo context, but the blur treatment is clean enough it could work as an actual in-app privacy mode

## Notes
- Header-as-hero: put the most emotionally significant number in the brand color zone
- Net-per-day in section headers = brilliant information density trick
- No red debits → fintech anxiety reduction pattern worth stealing
- Pill account switcher: small but powerful for multi-account users
- Inline `+` next to balance = action in context, not in toolbar
- Two-column spend card inside header = dashboard-within-dashboard
- Consider blurred names as a real privacy toggle, not just demo artifact
- Icon + currency badge combo solves FX transaction identification cleanly

## Screen
A lesson completion / reward screen from an educational or skill-building app. This is a celebratory interstitial that appears after a user finishes a learning unit. Single-column, vertically centered layout with a hero illustration in the upper half, reward data in the middle, and a single CTA anchored to the bottom.

## Layout & Spacing
Content is divided into roughly three zones: large illustration occupying ~45% of screen height, text/reward block centered in the middle third, and a full-width CTA at the bottom. Generous white space between each zone creates breathing room and lets the celebration moment land. The XP number and label are tightly grouped as a unit, separated from the headline by noticeable gap — reinforcing hierarchy through spacing alone.

## Components
- **Hero illustration**: 3D-style floating badge/gem above a glowing blue platform with concentric rings and a checkmark. Soft light beam radiates upward. Feels dimensional without being photorealistic.
- **Headline text**: "Lesson Complete!" in heavy black weight, centered, two lines.
- **XP label**: Small all-caps gray label "TOTAL XP" sitting above the number — functions as a micro-label/eyebrow.
- **XP number**: Oversized bold numeral "175" paired with a small green sparkle icon inline.
- **CTA button**: Full-width, dark charcoal/near-black pill button with white label "Continue." High contrast, rounded corners (~50px radius).

## Typography & Color
- **Type hierarchy**: Large bold headline → small all-caps muted label → oversized numeral → button label. Clean two-weight system (bold + regular).
- **Color palette**: Near-white background, dark charcoal for headline and button, medium gray for the XP label, bright green-yellow gradient on the badge, blue/periwinkle for the platform glow, green accent sparkle.
- **Color function**: Green signals reward/positive reinforcement. Blue platform creates depth. Dark button creates strong affordance contrast against the light screen. Color is otherwise restrained — celebration comes from illustration, not UI chrome.

## Patterns
- **Floating 3D trophy/badge pattern**: The reward object hovers above a glowing platform — implies levitation/achievement without literal trophy metaphor. The light beam creates vertical energy drawing the eye upward then back down.
- **Inline icon as unit suffix**: The sparkle ✦ icon placed inline next to the XP number makes the reward feel "live" and gamified without adding a separate row or label.
- **Separation of label and value**: "TOTAL XP" as a tiny eyebrow above the large numeral creates a data-card feel within a celebration screen — borrows from dashboard design language.
- **Restraint in celebration**: No confetti, no animation implied in static — the illustration carries all the emotional weight. This avoids visual noise while still feeling rewarding.
- **Dark CTA on light screen**: Counterintuitive choice (most apps use colored primary buttons) — the dark pill feels premium and calm, avoiding the typical green/blue "success" button that would compete with the reward illustration.
- **Massive vertical whitespace below XP**: The large empty zone between the XP block and the CTA creates a pause — mimics the feeling of letting a moment sink in before moving on.

## Notes
- 3D platform + floating object = reusable reward moment pattern, swap badge for any achievement icon
- Eyebrow label + giant number = instant data-card in celebration context
- Dark pill CTA as "calm" primary — works when illustration already carries the energy
- Inline sparkle suffix on numbers — tiny detail that elevates gamification feel
- White space as emotional pacing — don't rush the user to the CTA
- Green-to-yellow gradient on badge = "alive" feeling vs flat icon
- Concentric ring platform = depth cue, cheap 3D without full 3D render cost

## Screen
Home/landing screen of a fast-casual food ordering app. This is the first screen a logged-in user sees — a promotional hero moment combined with quick-access navigation. Full-bleed hero layout with overlaid UI elements, sitting at the top of the main app flow.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Full-bleed food photography occupies nearly the entire viewport — no chrome interrupts the image until the bottom nav. Text and CTAs float over the image in the top-left quadrant, creating a magazine-editorial feel. A persistent rewards pill anchors to the bottom-left of the hero, just above the nav bar, creating a layered z-depth effect. Utility icons (profile, QR scanner) stack vertically on the top-right, keeping them accessible without competing with the headline.

---

## Components
- **Hero image**: Edge-to-edge, no border radius on the image itself — bleeds into the phone frame
- **Eyebrow label**: Small all-caps tracking text above headline, low visual weight, acts as a collaboration/context tag
- **Headline**: Large bold serif-adjacent sans, white, high contrast over dark food photography
- **CTA pill button**: Lime/chartreuse yellow-green, rounded pill shape, sits below headline — high contrast accent against dark background
- **Icon buttons (top-right)**: Circular frosted/white background, outline-style icons — profile avatar and QR code scanner stacked vertically
- **Rewards floating pill**: White pill with branded hex logo mark + label, positioned over the hero image just above the nav — feels like a persistent widget
- **Bottom nav**: Clean 5-item tab bar with icon + label; Rewards tab has a small orange notification dot

---

## Typography & Color
- **Type hierarchy**: Collaboration eyebrow (small caps, tracking, white) → Hero headline (large, bold, white) → CTA button text (medium weight, dark)
- **Color palette**: Dark, moody food photography as the dominant visual; lime/chartreuse green as the single accent color for the primary CTA; white for all overlaid text and icon containers; orange dot as a micro-notification accent
- **Functional color use**: Chartreuse CTA pops against the dark image without feeling out of place — warm food tones make cool yellow-green feel energetic; white containers on icons ensure legibility regardless of image content beneath

---

## Patterns
- **Hero-as-canvas**: The entire screen is treated as editorial real estate — UI elements are placed *on* the image rather than the image being placed in a container. This elevates the food photography to brand storytelling.
- **Floating persistent widget**: The rewards pill sitting above the nav bar is a clever middle ground — it's not nav, not a modal, not a card. It persists as a contextual shortcut without taking up layout space.
- **Stacked utility icons**: Profile + QR scanner vertically stacked top-right keeps secondary functions reachable without a traditional header bar. Avoids the visual noise of a full top nav.
- **Eyebrow + headline collaboration pattern**: "Brand X Brand" eyebrow above a product name headline is borrowed from editorial/fashion — signals premium positioning and limited-time urgency without a badge or timer.
- **Notification dot placement**: Small orange dot on the Rewards nav icon creates pull toward that tab without an intrusive badge count — restrained but effective.
- **No visible search bar on home**: Deliberately omits search at the top level, pushing users toward curated content first — editorial over utilitarian.

---

## Notes
- Full-bleed hero = brand moment, not just a banner
- Lime CTA on dark food photo = high contrast without neon feeling
- Floating pill widget pattern — steal for loyalty/status nudges
- Stacked icon buttons top-right = cleaner than hamburger or full header
- Eyebrow "collab" text → editorial voice, creates FOMO/urgency passively
- QR scanner surfaced at home level = in-store use case baked into main flow
- Rewards dot = softest possible notification treatment, worth noting

## Screen
Home dashboard for a telehealth/prescription delivery app. This is the logged-in home state — a personalized hub showing order status, active subscriptions, and upsell content. Sits at the top of the main navigation flow. Layout is a single-column vertical scroll with distinct card sections stacked below a greeting header.

## Layout & Spacing
Content is divided into three clear zones: (1) personalized greeting + order status card, (2) subscription management card, (3) promotional discovery card. Generous white space between cards creates breathing room and visual separation without dividers. Cards have rounded corners and subtle shadows. Internal card padding is consistent and comfortable — roughly 16–20px. The promotional image card bleeds to near full-width, creating a visual shift in density that signals "content" vs "utility."

## Components
- **Greeting header** — Large bold two-line text, left-aligned. Profile avatar icon floats top-right as a circular button.
- **Order status card** — White card with product thumbnail (right-aligned), status text + subtitle, and a horizontal progress bar with labeled steps below it. Progress bar uses purple fill against a light gray track.
- **Subscription card** — White card with product thumbnail + name + next refill date, followed by two side-by-side action buttons, then a full-width CTA button.
- **Refill Now button** — Ghost/outline style with low visual weight; icon prefix. Signals secondary action.
- **Manage button** — Outlined with slightly more weight, gear icon prefix. Peer-level secondary action.
- **Add Treatment CTA** — Full-width, black pill button with "+" prefix. High contrast, dominant.
- **Promotional card** — Warm tan/brown background with a category label pill ("Weight Loss") in warm orange. Product photography fills the card.

## Typography & Color
- **Type hierarchy**: Large bold sans-serif for greeting (~28–30px), medium bold for card titles (~16–18px), small regular for subtitles/dates (~13–14px), small caps-style labels for progress steps.
- **Color palette**: Near-white background (#F5F5F5 range), white cards, black for primary text and CTA button, purple accent for progress bar fill, warm orange for category pill, tan/brown for promo card background.
- **Color function**: Purple is used exclusively for progress/status indication. Black signals the primary action. Orange signals category/discovery content. Muted grays carry secondary text.

## Patterns
- **Inline order tracking inside a home card** — Rather than requiring navigation to a separate tracking screen, the 4-step progress bar is embedded directly in the dashboard. Reduces friction for the most anxiety-prone part of a healthcare order flow.
- **Asymmetric button pairing** — "Refill Now" is visually de-emphasized (ghost, lighter weight) while "Manage" gets slightly more presence. This subtly discourages impulsive refills while keeping the option accessible — smart for a subscription health product.
- **Dominant upsell CTA inside subscription card** — "Add Treatment" lives inside the subscription section, not as a separate section. This contextually anchors upsell to existing relationship rather than feeling like an ad.
- **Warm editorial photography for discovery** — The promotional card uses lifestyle/product photography with a warm color-matched background, creating a magazine-like feel that contrasts with the clinical utility of the upper cards. Signals a tonal shift from "managing health" to "exploring options."
- **Category pill on promo card** — Small floating label ("Weight Loss") in warm orange positions the card as browsable content, not a hard sell. Feels editorial.
- **Greeting as emotional anchor** — Personalized name in oversized type at the top sets a warm, human tone before presenting clinical/transactional content below.

## Notes
- Progress bar with text labels below = great for multi-step status without needing a separate tracking page
- Ghost button for "risky" action (refill = cost/commitment), outlined for neutral action — button weight as behavioral nudge
- Upsell CTA inside existing product card = feels like a natural extension, not an ad
- Warm photo card as visual palette cleanser between utility sections
- Purple = status/progress only — tight color discipline
- Two-line large greeting = emotional warmth before clinical content, worth copying for health/finance apps
- Product thumbnail repeated in both order card and subscription card = reinforces product identity, builds familiarity

## Screen
AI chat interface on mobile, mid-conversation state. The screen captures the moment an AI response is actively being generated (streaming). Sits within an active conversation flow. Layout: top nav bar, scrollable message area, fixed bottom input bar.

## Layout & Spacing
Generous vertical whitespace in the message area — the emptiness below the streaming response is intentional, representing in-progress generation rather than a design gap. User message is right-aligned with comfortable horizontal padding. AI response is left-aligned, flush to the left margin without a bubble container. Input bar is cleanly separated at the bottom with clear breathing room between the text field and icon row.

## Components
- **User message bubble**: Rounded pill-like container, light gray fill, no hard shadow, comfortable internal padding. Right-aligned.
- **AI response text**: No bubble — raw text directly on white background. Uses inline bold for key phrases mid-sentence.
- **Streaming fade effect**: The AI response text trails off with a gray fade ("creaminess" appears lighter), visually communicating live generation in progress.
- **Input bar**: Minimal — placeholder text "Ask anything" in muted gray, no visible border or box. Icon row below with +, sliders/filter icon, mic, and a stop (square) button.
- **Stop button**: Filled black circle with white square inside — prominent, high-contrast, clearly the primary action during generation.
- **Nav bar**: Centered model name with a disclosure chevron (suggesting switchability), hamburger-style menu left, edit/compose icon right.

## Typography & Color
Near-monochromatic palette — white, light gray, near-black. No accent colors. Type hierarchy: nav title is medium weight, AI response body uses bold inline for emphasis within regular weight text. The fading gray on streaming text is a functional color use — communicating incompleteness. Placeholder text is muted gray, receding appropriately.

## Patterns
- **Inline bold as semantic emphasis**: Rather than using headers or separate UI elements, key terms in the AI response are bolded inline — mirrors how a knowledgeable person would speak, feels natural rather than structured.
- **Text fade as streaming indicator**: Instead of a spinner or loading bar, the response text itself fades out mid-sentence — the UI communicates state through content degradation, not a separate indicator element.
- **Bubble vs. no-bubble asymmetry**: User messages get a container bubble; AI responses don't. This creates a clear conversational hierarchy — the AI's output feels like the "environment" while the user's input feels like a discrete action.
- **Stop button prominence during generation**: The send button transforms into a high-contrast stop button (black circle) — the primary affordance shifts contextually without layout change.
- **Input bar icon density**: Four icons in the toolbar — attachment, settings/filters, mic, and stop — kept minimal with no labels, trusting iconography. Mic and stop are right-clustered as primary voice/control actions.
- **Model name as tappable nav element**: Chevron after the model name in the nav bar signals switchability — a subtle but powerful affordance for power users without cluttering the interface.

## Notes
- Fade-out text as loading state = elegant, content-native progress indicator
- Asymmetric bubble treatment (user = bubble, AI = raw text) worth stealing
- Stop button as contextual primary CTA swap — no layout shift
- Inline bold for AI emphasis feels conversational, not document-like
- Whitespace below streaming text = don't fill empty states during generation
- Model switcher in nav title = power feature hidden in plain sight
- Monochrome palette keeps focus entirely on content/text

## Screen
Flight tracking app showing a live flight status screen. The screen sits at the main dashboard/home level after a user has added a flight. Split composition: full-bleed 3D globe visualization occupying ~70% of the screen, with a bottom sheet panel containing the flight list and search. The globe shows the actual flight arc route rendered as a glowing blue curve over a realistic Earth render.

## Layout & Spacing
Two-zone layout: immersive map/globe fills the top portion edge-to-edge with no chrome interruption, then a bottom sheet with rounded top corners floats over it. The sheet uses generous internal padding (~20px sides). Flight row uses a three-column micro-layout: large duration on left, route details in center, status on right. Vertical rhythm in the flight row is tight but readable — airline code, city names, and airport codes each on their own line.

## Components
- **Globe/Map view**: Full-bleed 3D Earth render with night-side shading, city lights, and a glowing arc line showing the flight path with endpoint dots
- **Floating icon toolbar** (top right): Two stacked pill/square buttons with map and weather icons — white icons on dark frosted background, rounded corners
- **Bottom sheet**: White card with large top-left radius, sits over the globe like a drawer in resting position
- **Search bar**: Full-width, lightly stroked, rounded rectangle with leading search icon — subdued placeholder text
- **Section title with chevron**: "My Flights" in bold with a dropdown chevron suggesting switchable views
- **Share + avatar buttons**: Top right of sheet, icon-only, circular avatar placeholder
- **Flight row**: Compact data-dense card — no explicit card border, just whitespace separation. Duration displayed in very large numerals with "MINUTES" in small caps below

## Typography & Color
Strong size contrast: flight duration "5h" is displayed at ~40px bold, creating an immediate anchor. "41 MINUTES" below in ~10px all-caps gray creates a supporting label hierarchy. Route "Jakarta to Seoul" is medium weight ~16px. Airport codes (CGK, ICN) are smaller, paired with times in medium weight. Color is used functionally: "On Time" status in green, departure/arrival times in green matching the status — green = good/confirmed. Airline code and secondary labels in muted gray. Overall palette: black space/globe, white sheet, green for positive status, gray for secondary info.

## Patterns
- **Globe as functional visualization, not decoration**: The 3D Earth actually shows the flight arc geographically — the map IS the data, not a background image
- **Dramatic scale contrast for duration**: Using oversized numerals for flight time (5h) with tiny supporting label creates an at-a-glance read from distance
- **Color-coded status inline with times**: Green applied to both "On Time" label AND the departure/arrival times creates a visual link — the times themselves feel confirmed/live
- **Bottom sheet as persistent HUD**: The sheet doesn't cover the globe fully — intentional partial reveal keeps the spatial context visible while surfacing actionable info
- **+1 day indicator**: Small superscript "+1" next to arrival time is an elegant, minimal solution to overnight/next-day arrivals without adding a separate label
- **Stacked floating toolbar**: Map/weather toggle buttons stacked vertically in top corner — layer controls that don't interrupt the immersive view
- **Airport code + time pairing with icon**: Small circular airline-branded icon before each airport code creates scannable departure/arrival anchors

## Notes
- Globe = data layer, not hero image — steal this for any geo-based tracking
- Oversized single metric (5h) as anchor → tiny supporting label below in caps
- Green on both status badge AND times = color links related info across the row
- Bottom sheet resting state shows ~30% — teases content, keeps map visible
- +1 superscript for next-day arrival is cleaner than writing "next day"
- Stacked icon toolbar top-right = layer switcher without modal overhead
- Night-side Earth render makes the glowing flight arc pop — dark bg = data visibility

## Screen
Online learning/video content platform home screen. This is the main discovery/browse screen — the entry point after login. Layout follows a vertical scroll with a full-bleed hero carousel at top, horizontal category filter tabs below the nav, and content rows beneath. Primary interactive elements: category tabs, hero card (play + save actions), and a secondary content row beginning to appear at scroll.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Hero card dominates ~60% of the visible screen height, creating strong visual hierarchy. Cards in the carousel bleed off both edges, signaling horizontal swipe affordance. Below the hero, a section header with a horizontal thumbnail strip begins — classic Netflix-style row pattern. Tab bar sits flush to the screen edge with generous tap targets. Content has tight left alignment with consistent padding (~16–20px margins).

---

## Components
- **Category filter tabs** — pill/capsule shape; active state uses white fill with dark text, inactive states are plain text on dark background, no border. Subtle but clear contrast.
- **Hero card** — full-bleed photo with bottom gradient overlay fading to black. Large bold title text overlaid directly on image.
- **Play button** — solid white pill with play icon + label; high contrast CTA.
- **My List button** — outlined/ghost pill with "+" icon; secondary action visually subordinate to Play.
- **Bottom nav** — icon + label, three items; center item appears slightly highlighted/active with subtle warm tint behind it.
- **Thumbnail row** — small cards with circular portrait image + additional card types visible, hinting at mixed content formats.

---

## Typography & Color
- **Title on hero**: Extra-bold, condensed/display weight, all-caps — very large, cinematic feel
- **Subtitle**: Smaller, sentence case, medium weight — sits below title for context
- **Tab labels**: Medium weight, modest size
- **Color palette**: Near-black background (#0a0a0a), white text, coral/pink-red brand accent in logo. Buttons use white-on-dark and outline-on-dark. The blue in the hero photo creates an incidental but complementary color moment against the dark UI.
- Color is used functionally: white fill = primary action, ghost outline = secondary action, active tab = white fill vs. inactive plain text.

---

## Patterns
- **Bleeding carousel edges** — partial visibility of adjacent cards on both sides communicates swipeability without any explicit instruction
- **Dual CTA on hero** — Play (filled) + My List (ghost) creates clear action hierarchy without stacking or overwhelming; both fit on one line
- **Gradient-to-black image overlay** — allows white text to remain legible over any photo without a separate text container; seamlessly merges card into app background
- **All-caps display type on hero** — editorial/magazine treatment elevates perceived content quality; feels premium rather than utilitarian
- **Category tabs as content filter, not navigation** — tabs don't change pages, they filter the feed; keeps user in flow
- **Mixed card types in content row** — circular portrait card alongside rectangular cards suggests different content types (instructor profile vs. lesson/episode), giving visual rhythm to the row

---

## Notes
- Ghost + filled pill button pair = clean dual-CTA pattern worth stealing
- All-caps condensed font on hero = instant premium signal
- Bleed-edge carousel: never show only one card, always tease neighbors
- Dark bg + gradient overlay = zero-cost text legibility on any image
- Active tab: white fill pill feels more "selected" than underline — consider for content-heavy dark UIs
- Bottom nav warm tint on active item = subtle but effective without heavy highlight
- Section title above horizontal scroll row = good anchor before content flood

## Screen
Health/fitness tracking app — activity detail screen. Sits within a dashboard or metric drill-down flow, likely accessed by tapping an activity card from a home screen. Layout flows top-to-bottom: dismiss control → hero illustration → score section → measurements list. Modal-style presentation (X button suggests overlay).

## Layout & Spacing
Full-bleed hero illustration takes roughly 45% of screen height — unusually generous for data content. Content below uses a warm cream background with a white card inset for the score. Generous padding (~20px) on all content sections. The score card uses internal padding that gives the large number room to breathe. "Latest Measurements" section begins to peek at the bottom, signaling scrollability.

## Components
- **X/dismiss button** — small circular white pill, top-left, subtle shadow, sits above the hero
- **Hero illustration** — full-width, edge-to-edge, rounded bottom corners blend into background; no hard border
- **Premium badge** — small outlined pill/chip with border, no fill, positioned just below hero as a content gate signal
- **Section header + info icon** — dark olive text with a small circular info button (ⓘ) right-aligned on same row
- **Score card** — white rounded rectangle on cream background; contains label, oversized numeral, denominator in smaller weight, and a delta/trend line with an upward arrow icon inline with text
- **Chart icon button** — small dark circular icon button, bottom-right of score card
- **Measurements row** — list item with activity icon, label, dot pagination indicator (5 dots), timestamp, and chevron

## Typography & Color
- **Display numeral** ("22") — very large, bold, dark olive/brown; creates immediate hierarchy
- **"out of 100"** — same line, significantly smaller, regular weight — classic pairing of scale contrast on one line
- **Section headers** — medium-large, bold, dark brown
- **Body/description text** — small, muted brown, relaxed line height
- **Palette**: Warm cream/yellow (#F5E9A0 range) as dominant background; dark olive-brown for text; white for cards; orange accent (sun in illustration echoes the premium badge warmth); muted blue-purple in illustration as the only cool note
- Color is used functionally: white card = interactive/data zone; cream = ambient/context zone

## Patterns
- **Illustration as emotional framing** — the hero isn't decorative filler; it sets the activity type (cycling) and creates an aspirational, lifestyle tone before showing a low score (22/100), softening potentially discouraging data
- **Score contextualization inline** — "out of 100" is typographically subordinate but spatially adjacent to the number, avoiding the need for a separate label row
- **Delta with directional icon in prose** — trend change ("increased by ↗ 2 points") is written as a sentence with an inline icon rather than a separate stat chip — feels conversational, reduces cognitive load
- **Premium gating via badge placement** — the subscription badge sits between the illustration and the score, implying the score itself is a premium feature without blocking it outright; soft upsell
- **Dot pagination on list item** — unusual to see pagination dots inside a list row; suggests the measurement item itself is swipeable/has multiple data points without leaving the list context
- **Chart icon as secondary action** — rather than a full "View Chart" button, a small icon button in the card corner keeps the action available without visual weight

## Notes
- Hero illustration softens bad metrics — consider for any score/grade reveal screen
- Inline delta as prose > separate stat chip for friendliness
- Cream bg + white card = clear data hierarchy without borders
- Premium badge between content sections = non-blocking soft gate pattern
- Dot indicator inside list row = swipeable sub-data without navigation
- Big number + small denominator same baseline line = efficient score display
- Info ⓘ icon paired with section title = good pattern for explaining algorithmic scores

## Screen
Outdoor trail discovery app — explore/browse screen. This is the home feed, likely the first screen after onboarding or the default landing state. Layout is a vertical scroll feed of trail cards beneath a search bar and horizontal filter chips. Primary interactions: search, filter selection, card tap, bookmark/download actions.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Top-anchored search bar with full-width treatment. Filter chips sit directly below with generous horizontal padding, scrollable off-screen (third chip clips). Cards are full-bleed width with ~16px side margins. Metadata row below card image is compact but well-spaced using dot separators. Vertical rhythm between card image and text feels tight-but-intentional — no excess whitespace between photo and title.

---

## Components
- **Search bar**: Rounded pill shape, light gray fill, left-aligned placeholder with search icon, filter/sort icon button on right end — contained within the bar itself
- **Filter chips**: Rounded rectangle, outlined style for inactive, filled dark (near-black) with white text for active state; each chip has a small custom illustrated icon — not generic SF symbols
- **Trail card image**: Full-width, tall aspect ratio (~4:3), rounded corners; hosts two overlaid action buttons
- **Bookmark button** (top-right of image): White circular button, bookmark icon — clean floating affordance
- **Download button** (top-right of second card): Circular white button with download icon — same pattern
- **Mini map thumbnail**: Rounded square inset in bottom-right of image — shows trail route preview, feels like a secondary peek
- **Map pill button**: Bright green pill with map icon and "Map" label — floats over second card image, high contrast CTA
- **Metadata row**: Star rating + difficulty label + distance + estimated time, separated by centered dots, small gray text

---

## Typography & Color
- Title text: Medium-weight, dark, ~16–17px — truncated with ellipsis for long names
- Location: Small, muted gray, secondary hierarchy
- Metadata: Same small size, gray, all inline — no label prefixes, relies on icon + value convention
- Color palette: Near-white background, dark forest green/black for active states and icons, bright lime-green for the Map CTA, white for floating buttons — earthy/nature-coded palette without being heavy-handed

---

## Patterns

**Embedded map thumbnail on card**: Rather than a separate "view map" step, a miniature route map is inset directly into the hero image corner. This gives spatial context without leaving the feed — a progressive disclosure of detail.

**Floating contextual CTA over image**: The green "Map" pill button overlaid on the second card's photo creates a direct shortcut to a different view mode without requiring a tap into the detail page first. Interrupts the scroll with a mode-switch affordance.

**Illustrated chip icons**: Filter chips use small custom character-style illustrations (people, animals) rather than standard icons — adds personality and aids quick scanning beyond just reading the label.

**Dot-separated inline metadata**: Rating · difficulty · distance · time all on one line with dot separators — maximally dense without feeling cluttered. No labels, assumes literacy from context.

**Dual action buttons on card**: Bookmark (save) and Download are both accessible directly from the feed card without entering detail view — reduces friction for power users who browse and queue content.

**Active filter chip uses dark fill**: The selected state inverts to near-black fill — strong contrast signal, more emphatic than a simple underline or color tint.

---

## Notes
- Mini map inset on photo = genius progressive detail — steal this for any content with spatial/geographic data
- Lime green pill CTA floating over image = mode-switch without nav — consider for view toggles (list ↔ map)
- Illustrated filter icons > generic icons for personality — worth the asset cost
- Dot separator for metadata rows: compact, scannable, no label overhead
- Download + save both surfaced at card level = power user optimization
- Search bar filter icon embedded inside bar = saves vertical space vs. separate row
- Dark active chip state feels more decisive than typical blue/tint selection patterns

## Screen
Active focus/productivity timer screen in a distraction-blocking app. This is the "in-session" state — the screen a user sees while a focus session is running. Layout splits into two zones: a full-bleed hero image occupying roughly the top 55%, and a dark content panel below containing session metadata, controls, and actions.

---

## Layout & Spacing

Two-zone composition: immersive photo header bleeds edge-to-edge with a natural gradient fade into near-black at the bottom. Content panel sits on pure black, creating a clean stage for white text. The transition between photo and panel is a soft gradient scrim rather than a hard edge. Info cards sit in a 2-column grid with equal gutters. Primary CTA sits with generous vertical padding above a secondary destructive text link. Tight label/value pairing on the timer line keeps scanning fast.

---

## Components

- **Hero image with gradient scrim** — Full-width landscape photo fades to black via a bottom gradient; creates mood without competing with text
- **Floating "Edit Session" pill** — Small dark pill button with pencil icon, positioned bottom-right of the image zone; low-prominence but accessible
- **Timer progress bar** — Thin horizontal scrubber-style bar with a white thumb indicator; start/end times labeled at each end; minimal, clock-like
- **Info cards (2-col grid)** — Dark gray rounded rectangles (~12px radius); left card shows block list with emoji + icon metadata row; right card shows difficulty with label/value stacked and a chevron for drill-down
- **Snooze CTA** — Full-width pill button with a soft green-to-cyan gradient fill; high contrast against black background; rounded pill shape
- **"Leave Early" text button** — Red/coral text-only link; no container; communicates destructive/escape action through color alone

---

## Typography & Color

**Type hierarchy:**
- Session title: large, bold white (~24px)
- "Remaining time" label: medium gray, regular weight; time value in white monospace-style for readability
- Card labels: small all-caps gray ("DIFFICULTY", "BLOCK LIST"); values in white medium weight
- Metadata row: small icons + numbers in muted gray

**Color palette:**
- Background: pure black (#000)
- Surface cards: dark gray (~#1C1C1E)
- Primary action: green-to-cyan gradient (calm, go-signal)
- Destructive action: coral/red text only
- Accent: emoji red circle on block list card adds warmth
- All text: white or mid-gray on dark; no ambiguity

---

## Patterns

**Aspirational wallpaper as motivational UX** — The nature landscape isn't decorative; it sets a psychological tone of calm focus. The image choice does emotional work that copy can't.

**Gradient scrim as content transition** — Instead of a card sliding over the image, the image dissolves into the content area. Feels cinematic, not utilitarian.

**Destructive action as text-only** — "Leave Early" has no button container, only red color. This creates friction through visual de-emphasis without hiding the option — honest but discouraging.

**Gradient CTA as mood signal** — The green-to-mint gradient on Snooze reads as "safe/okay" rather than urgent. Color does emotional signaling, not just affordance.

**Timer as timeline, not countdown** — Showing start time + end time flanking a progress bar reframes the session as a bounded journey rather than a ticking clock. Reduces anxiety.

**Floating contextual edit** — "Edit Session" appears as a ghost pill over the image, not in the nav. Keeps the primary surface clean while preserving access.

**Emoji as category signal** — Red circle emoji on the block list card is a fast visual anchor that breaks the monochrome card grid without adding a custom icon system.

---

## Notes

- Scrim-to-black transition > hard card edge — feels premium
- Timer as start→end timeline = reframe anxiety into progress
- Gradient button = "this is okay" vs solid red = "danger"
- Destructive actions: no container + color = friction without hiding
- Nature wallpaper as emotional UX layer — worth testing vs abstract
- 2-col info cards: label ALL CAPS small + value white = fast scan
- Floating pill edit button over hero = contextual without nav clutter
- Black bg makes gradient CTA pop with zero extra effort

## Screen
Health/fitness tracking app — body composition detail screen. Sits mid-flow after selecting a specific metric from a dashboard. Shows historical trend data with a time-range selector, an interactive chart, summary delta cards, and a breakdown section below. Scrollable detail view.

---

## Layout & Spacing

Top-to-bottom hierarchy: nav bar → time filter tabs → date navigator → tooltip/summary overlay → line chart → delta summary cards → section header → breakdown cards (horizontal scroll). Content lives in a light card container that floats on a slightly warm off-white background. The chart section takes up roughly 40% of the visible screen — generous real estate signaling it's the primary data object. Summary delta cards below use a 2-column equal grid with comfortable internal padding (~16px). Breakdown section uses horizontal scroll cards, partially revealing the next card to signal more content.

---

## Components

- **Segmented time filter (underline style):** Four options (Week/Month/Quarter/Year), active state shown with a filled pill/highlight behind "Month" in soft lavender-purple. No border on inactive items.
- **Date navigator:** Chevron buttons flanking a centered "This Month" label — left chevron is active (dark), right is muted (disabled state implied by lighter fill).
- **Tooltip card (floating overlay on chart):** Appears anchored to the selected data point. Contains metric label, average header, three inline percentage values with labels (Muscle/Fat/Bone), a date stamp, and a chevron to expand. Card has subtle shadow and rounded corners.
- **Line chart:** Multi-line, minimal gridlines, right-side Y-axis labels. Vertical crosshair line drops from selected point to X-axis. Data points rendered as filled circles. One line notably flat (bone), one trending upward (muscle).
- **Delta summary cards:** Rounded rectangle cards, white fill, two per row. Large bold delta value (+3.7%, -3.6%) with metric label above in smaller weight.
- **Breakdown cards (horizontal scroll):** White cards with metric name, info icon (dark circle with ?), RESULT label + status (green checkmark + "Healthy"), TREND label + numeric value.

---

## Typography & Color

**Type hierarchy:**
- Screen title: small caps, tracked-out, medium weight
- Section headers ("Body Composition Breakdown"): large, serif-adjacent weight, left-aligned
- Data values: bold, large — dominant visual weight
- Labels (AVERAGE, RESULT, TREND): all-caps, small, muted gray — clear secondary tier
- Delta values: large bold, no unit styling distinction

**Color palette:**
- Background: warm light gray (#F2F2F4 range)
- Cards: white
- Accent/active: soft lavender-purple (tab highlight, trend icon)
- Positive metric: teal/mint green (muscle line, checkmark icons)
- Neutral lines: light gray
- Text: near-black primary, medium gray secondary
- Color used functionally: green = healthy/positive, purple = selected/active state, muted = inactive/disabled

---

## Patterns

- **Floating tooltip as summary card:** Rather than a separate stats section, the data summary appears as a persistent overlay anchored to the chart's active point — blurs the line between chart interaction and data display. Keeps context tight.
- **Inline multi-metric comparison in tooltip:** Three metrics (Muscle/Fat/Bone %) shown horizontally in one tooltip row with a "more" chevron — compresses multi-dimensional data without requiring separate views.
- **Positive/negative framing via sign:** Delta cards use explicit +/- signs on values rather than color alone to communicate direction — accessibility-conscious.
- **Partial card reveal for horizontal scroll:** Breakdown section bleeds the second card off-screen, communicating scrollability without any explicit affordance or label.
- **Disabled nav state via visual weight:** Right chevron on date navigator appears lighter/grayed — communicates "future unavailable" without a tooltip or lock icon.
- **Result + Trend as paired data pattern:** Each breakdown card separates RESULT (categorical: Healthy) from TREND (numeric: -0.3) — smart split between status and momentum.

---

## Notes

- Tooltip-as-persistent-summary is a strong pattern — steal for any chart detail screen
- All-caps micro-labels for data categories = clean hierarchy without size difference
- Lavender as "selected" accent feels fresh vs. typical blue — consider for health/wellness contexts
- Horizontal card scroll for breakdown = avoids long scroll, creates discovery feel
- +/- sign on delta values alongside color = redundant encoding done right
- Date navigator with disabled future state is a small but polished detail
- Consider: tooltip card with expand chevron for progressive disclosure of chart data

## Screen
Outdoor trail/hiking discovery app — the main explore/browse feed. This is the home entry point where users find trails near them. Layout is a vertical scroll feed with a sticky search bar + filter chips at top, followed by full-width trail cards stacked vertically.

## Layout & Spacing
Top section: full-width search bar with inline filter icon. Below it, a horizontally scrollable chip row. Then the feed begins with edge-to-edge cards (no side margins on the images). Text metadata sits below each image with comfortable padding. The card image takes up roughly 60% of the card height, creating a photo-first hierarchy. Generous whitespace between the image bottom and the next card start.

## Components
- **Search bar**: Pill-shaped, light gray fill, left-aligned placeholder with search icon, filter/sliders icon on the right end — clean two-action bar
- **Filter chips**: Rounded rectangle pills with custom illustrated icons (not standard iconography — small character/scene illustrations). Active state uses a dark border/outline to distinguish from inactive. Horizontally scrollable, partially cropped rightmost chip signals more options
- **Trail card image**: Full-bleed, rounded corners, with two overlaid elements — a bookmark icon (top right, white circle button) and a mini map thumbnail (bottom right, rounded square with trail route preview)
- **Download button**: Circle icon button overlaid on second card image, top right — consistent placement with bookmark
- **Metadata row**: Star rating + difficulty label + distance + estimated time — dot-separated inline string, compact single line
- **Map FAB**: Floating pill button overlaid on second card image — green background, map icon + "Map" label — persistent toggle to switch view modes

## Typography & Color
- Title text: medium-weight, dark, truncated with ellipsis — clear hierarchy
- Location text: smaller, muted gray — secondary info
- Metadata row: small, gray, icon-accented (star in yellow/gold)
- Color palette: white backgrounds, dark near-black text, green accent (chip icons, Map FAB), yellow star, muted grays for secondary text
- Green is used exclusively for interactive/action elements (active chip icons, Map FAB) — strong functional color coding

## Patterns
- **Mini map thumbnail embedded in card image** — gives spatial context without leaving the feed; the trail route preview is a clever progressive disclosure that reduces need to tap-to-explore
- **Illustrated chip icons** — custom scene illustrations on filter chips (not generic icons) adds personality and aids quick scanning by category feel rather than just label reading
- **Dual save actions**: bookmark (save to list) vs. download (offline access) as separate overlaid buttons — distinguishes two different user intents that are often conflated
- **Map FAB overlaid on card** — instead of a separate map toggle at the top level, it appears contextually on the card, suggesting "see this area on map" rather than a global view switch
- **Dot-separated metadata string** — packs rating + difficulty + distance + time into one scannable line without table/grid overhead
- **Partial chip clipping** — rightmost chip is intentionally half-visible, a classic affordance cue for horizontal scroll without any explicit indicator

## Notes
- Mini map preview inside card = genius spatial shorthand, steal this for any geo-content feed
- Illustrated filter chips >> generic icons for lifestyle/outdoor apps — adds warmth
- Green FAB floating over image = mode switch that feels in-context, not global
- Two distinct save intents (bookmark vs. download) worth separating explicitly
- Dot-separator metadata rows: compact, scannable, no label overhead
- Clip last chip to signal scroll — no arrows, no fade, just truncation

## Screen
A screen time / digital wellness dashboard — the home/summary screen of a focus or app-blocking app. Sits at the top of the navigation hierarchy as the primary daily overview. Layout flows vertically: header → hero visual → primary stat → secondary stats row → timeline chart → offline summary card → CTA → tab bar.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Content is organized in a clear top-to-bottom priority stack. The hero 3D object occupies roughly 35% of the screen height — an unusually generous allocation for a decorative/emotional element. Stats sit in a three-column grid below the primary number. The chart section uses full-width with labeled time axis. Bottom section transitions into a card-like row before the CTA. Generous breathing room between sections prevents density despite a lot of data.

---

## Components

- **3D Hero Object** — Photorealistic opal gemstone on a pedestal, softly lit with teal ambient glow radiating outward. Purely atmospheric, no interactivity implied.
- **Primary Stat Display** — Large numeral "1h 38m" in a warm yellow-green tint, with a small all-caps label below in muted white. High contrast against black.
- **Three-Column Stats Row** — "Most Used" shows actual app icons as data; "Focus Score" shows percentage; "Pickups" shows integer. Each column has a small all-caps label above the value.
- **Bar Chart** — Thin vertical bars, teal/mint for past hours, dark gray for future. One bar highlighted in red/coral to indicate a spike. Dotted horizontal reference line. Time labels below.
- **Time Offline Row** — Left-aligned icon + label, right-aligned value. Secondary percentage text below in smaller muted type. Feels like a list item / summary card.
- **CTA Button** — Full-width pill, gradient fill from mint to pale yellow-green. Play icon + "Block Now" text. Glows softly against the dark background.
- **Tab Bar** — Three items, minimal icon style, white active state, muted inactive.

---

## Typography & Color

**Type hierarchy:**
1. Hero number — very large, bold, warm yellow-green
2. Section values (Focus Score, Pickups) — medium bold, white
3. Labels — small, all-caps, tracked, muted gray
4. Supporting text (75% of your day) — small, subdued

**Color palette:**
- Background: near-pure black (#0a0a0a range)
- Accent/data: teal-mint (chart bars, glow, button gradient)
- Primary stat: warm yellow-green — emotionally distinct from the teal
- Alert/spike: coral-red (single bar in chart — used surgically as a warning signal)
- Text: white → light gray hierarchy

Color is used functionally: teal = neutral data, yellow-green = primary metric emphasis, red = behavioral alert.

---

## Patterns

- **Emotional mascot as state indicator** — The 3D gemstone likely changes appearance based on usage behavior (healthy vs. unhealthy screen time). Using a beautiful object rather than a score or face creates aspiration rather than shame. Deeply non-obvious motivational design.
- **App icons as data** — Using real app icons in the "Most Used" column instead of text or abstract icons is immediately scannable and personal. No label needed.
- **Single-bar anomaly highlighting** — The red bar in an otherwise uniform chart draws the eye instantly to the problematic time window without any tooltip or annotation needed. Minimal but effective data storytelling.
- **Future-time dimming** — Chart bars for upcoming hours are dark/empty, showing the day is still in progress. Implies agency — the rest of the day is still yours.
- **Gradient CTA as reward signal** — The mint-to-yellow gradient on "Block Now" mirrors the color language of the hero stat and gem glow, making the action feel aligned with positive outcomes rather than restriction.
- **"Time Offline" framing** — Reframes absence of phone use as a positive metric rather than showing only screen time. Psychological reframe built into the data architecture.

---

## Notes
- Gemstone as behavioral avatar — huge idea, steals from gaming/tamagotchi psychology
- Red single-bar = surgical use of color as alert, don't overuse
- App icons as chart labels > text labels always
- "Time Offline" positive reframe — copy pattern worth stealing for any wellness app
- Gradient pill CTA on dark bg = premium feel, works because rest of UI is restrained
- Yellow-green for hero number creates warmth against cold teal system — intentional emotional temperature split
- Generous hero image space signals confidence — not every px needs to be data

## Screen
A 1:1 messaging conversation screen within a mobile chat app. This is a mid-flow screen — the user has an existing conversation history. Layout is a standard chat thread: fixed header with contact info and call actions, scrollable message feed in the middle, and a persistent input bar at the bottom.

---

## Layout & Spacing

Messages are left/right aligned by sender with generous vertical breathing room between message clusters. Consecutive messages from the same sender stack tightly (minimal gap), while conversation turns have more separation — creating natural visual rhythm without explicit dividers. Date separators float centered in the feed. The input bar is clean and minimal, sitting flush at the bottom with a home indicator below.

---

## Components

- **Chat bubbles (sent):** Soft green fill, rounded corners, timestamp + read receipt embedded inside bubble at bottom-right
- **Chat bubbles (received):** White fill, same rounding, timestamp sits outside/beside the bubble in muted gray
- **Call event cards:** Wider pill/card format — same bubble width logic but with an icon (phone) on the left, bold label, and subdued metadata below. Missed call uses red icon as a state differentiator
- **Date separators:** Small pill with light background, centered, semibold small text
- **Encryption notice:** Centered card with lock icon, muted yellow-cream fill, inline bold "Learn more" CTA
- **Input bar:** Rounded text field, surrounded by utility icons (sticker, camera, mic, keyboard toggle)
- **Header:** Avatar (teal circle, initials/icon), bold name, video and phone call icons right-aligned

---

## Typography & Color

**Hierarchy:** Contact name (header) is largest/boldest → message body is regular weight, comfortable reading size → timestamps are smallest, muted gray, clearly subordinate  
**Color use:**
- Green bubbles = sent (functional color coding, not decorative)
- White bubbles = received
- Red phone icon = missed/failed call state (alert color used sparingly and meaningfully)
- Muted warm background texture (faint doodle pattern) adds depth without competing with content
- Teal avatar = contact identity anchor

---

## Patterns

- **Inline metadata inside bubbles:** Timestamp and read receipts live inside the sent bubble rather than below it — keeps the thread compact while preserving delivery status visibility
- **Call events as cards within the thread:** Voice calls are surfaced as interactive cards inline with messages, not separated into a call log — unifies communication history in one stream
- **Missed call as actionable card:** "Tap to call back" CTA embedded directly in the call card — reduces friction by surfacing recovery action exactly where the missed event appears
- **Timestamp asymmetry by sender:** Sent messages embed time inside the bubble; received messages show time outside — subtle but consistent rule that reinforces sender/receiver distinction beyond just alignment
- **Stacked short replies:** Short consecutive replies from the same sender stack with minimal gap, mimicking real conversational cadence and reducing visual noise
- **Security notice as ambient system message:** Encryption info is styled as a neutral, non-alarming card — informational but visually soft so it doesn't interrupt the conversation flow

---

## Notes

- Call cards in chat thread = great pattern for unified comms history
- Missed call → inline CTA is smart recovery UX, steal this
- Timestamp inside vs. outside bubble = subtle sender/receiver signal beyond alignment alone
- Read receipts (double checkmarks) as micro-status inside bubble — very low footprint
- Stacked short messages = conversational rhythm, don't over-separate
- Encryption notice: system info styled as ambient, not alarming — good model for trust/legal messaging
- Faint decorative background texture adds warmth without hurting readability — test this pattern
- Red used only for failure/missed state — color discipline pays off

## Screen
Workout logging entry form in a fitness/health tracking app. Sits at the manual workout addition step — likely accessed from a "+" or log action on a dashboard. Layout is a single-column scrollable form with a modal-style presentation (X dismiss, centered title). Primary interactive elements: chevron-based list rows, a custom effort slider, optional field rows, and a primary CTA button.

---

## Layout & Spacing

Content is grouped into three distinct card sections separated by visible gaps: (1) workout metadata rows, (2) perceived effort section with slider, (3) optional data fields. This chunking creates clear cognitive phases — what, when, how hard, extras. The effort section breaks the row pattern intentionally, expanding vertically to accommodate the slider visualization. Generous internal padding on all cards. CTA button is full-width with comfortable bottom spacing.

---

## Components

- **List rows with chevrons** — Label left, value+chevron right. Subtle dividers between rows within same card. Values are bold/dark to signal they're set.
- **"As Usual" pill badge** — Small rounded pill with a sparkle/AI icon prefix. Suggests smart default or ML-inferred suggestion. Outlined style, not filled.
- **Effort description block** — Bold label ("Moderate") + descriptive body text below. Humanizes the numeric scale with plain language.
- **Gradient effort slider** — Custom track using a spectral gradient (blue → yellow → orange → purple). Hatched/diagonal stripe pattern overlaid on the left portion marks "your usual range." A red pill label "ABOVE USUAL RANGE" floats above the thumb. White circular thumb with shadow.
- **Optional field rows** — Same row pattern but value text is muted gray "Optional" instead of a set value — clear visual state differentiation.
- **CTA button** — Full-width, rounded rectangle, warm orange fill. Two-line label: primary action bold ("Add") + secondary contextual line in smaller weight ("With Effort Above Your Usual Range").

---

## Typography & Color

- **Hierarchy**: Screen title (medium weight, centered) → Row labels (regular) → Row values (semibold/bold) → Section header "Moderate" (bold, larger) → Body description (regular, gray) → CTA primary (bold) / CTA sub-label (light/regular, smaller).
- **Color palette**: Near-white backgrounds, dark near-black text for set values, medium gray for labels and optional states. Accent: warm orange used exclusively for the CTA and the "above usual" zone on the slider — creates a coherent warning/attention color thread. Slider gradient spans cool-to-warm spectrum. Red used only for the "above usual range" floating pill — urgency signal.
- Color is doing functional work: orange = effort/exertion/attention, gray = unset/optional, red = out-of-norm alert.

---

## Patterns
