- **Contextual CTA label** — The button doesn't just say "Add" — it reflects the current state of the form ("With Effort Above Your Usual Range"). The button becomes a live summary of what you're about to commit to. Extremely useful for reducing confirmation anxiety.
- **Personalized range visualization on slider** — Rather than a generic 1–10 scale, the slider overlays the user's historical "usual range" as a hatched zone. The thumb position relative to personal history is more meaningful than an absolute number.
- **Floating anomaly label on slider** — The "ABOVE USUAL RANGE" pill attached to the slider thumb is a non-blocking inline alert. It informs without interrupting the flow — no modal, no toast.
- **AI/smart default pill inline with form label** — The "As Usual" suggestion sits right next to the section question, not below it or in a separate suggestion bar. Reduces friction while keeping the user in control.
- **Effort described in human language** — "Starting to feel the work, but still comfy enough to talk in shorter spurts" replaces a numeric label. Lowers cognitive load and makes the scale accessible to non-athletes.
- **Optional fields de-emphasized but present** — Active Energy and Distance are included but visually quieted. Doesn't hide them, doesn't demand them — respects varied user intent.

---

## Notes

- CTA as live form summary = killer pattern for any multi-field form
- Slider + personal range overlay → steal for any metric with historical context (sleep, mood, HRV)
- "Above usual" floating pill on thumb = inline anomaly detection, no interruption
- Humanize scales with prose descriptions, not just numbers
- Gradient track on slider communicates intensity spectrum intuitively
- "As Usual" AI chip inline with question label — feels like a whisper suggestion, not a takeover
- Optional fields in same list but muted gray value = elegant required vs optional treatment
- Hatched pattern = "your norm zone" — great for personalized baselines

## Screen
A payments home screen from a peer-to-peer money transfer app. This is the main landing screen after login, serving as both an onboarding/discovery surface and a quick-action hub. Layout is vertical stack: header with avatar, section title, a large horizontally-scrollable feature card, a balance summary card, and a persistent CTA button pair above the bottom nav.

## Layout & Spacing
Content is organized in distinct horizontal bands with generous vertical breathing room between sections. The hero feature card uses edge-to-edge width with visible card peeking on the right — signaling horizontal scroll. The balance card below sits inset with subtle shadow. The Send/Request buttons are pinned just above the nav bar, creating a persistent action zone separate from the scrollable content above.

## Components
- **Feature card (hero):** Large rounded-corner card in vivid blue, contains illustration, headline, body copy, and a white pill CTA button embedded within. Full-bleed feel with slight right-edge peek of next card.
- **Illustration cluster:** Three overlapping 3D-style objects (ball, shopping bag, coffee cup) — compositionally loose and lifestyle-oriented rather than iconographic.
- **Embedded CTA button:** White pill button inside the blue card — high contrast inversion. Semi-bold label, fully rounded.
- **Balance row card:** White card with logo mark, large dollar amount, and descriptor label. Minimal, data-forward.
- **Primary action buttons (Send/Request):** Warm golden-yellow, fully pill-shaped, equal width side-by-side. Semi-bold text in dark navy.
- **Bottom nav:** 3-tab, icon + label, active state in blue.

## Typography & Color
Clear three-level hierarchy: section title (medium weight, dark, ~18px), card headline (large, white, bold ~28px), card body copy (regular weight, white, ~15px). Balance amount uses large bold numerals for scannability.

Color palette: Royal blue (primary brand, card background), warm golden yellow (action buttons), white (text on blue, button on blue), dark navy (button labels, nav icons), light gray (page background). Color is used functionally — blue = informational/feature space, yellow = transactional actions. The two-tone system creates clear visual separation between content and action.

## Patterns
- **Card-as-mini-onboarding:** The hero card is essentially an inline feature education module — illustration + benefit copy + action — avoiding a separate onboarding flow by embedding discovery into the home screen.
- **Peek scroll affordance:** Partial visibility of a second card's edge signals horizontal swipeability without any explicit indicator. Subtle but effective.
- **Dual-register action zone:** The Send/Request buttons are architecturally separated from the scrollable content, floating persistently above the nav. This keeps primary actions always accessible regardless of scroll state.
- **Inverted button inside colored card:** White pill on blue background is a smart contrast inversion — the card's CTA doesn't compete with the yellow primary actions below, maintaining visual hierarchy across the whole screen.
- **Lifestyle illustration over iconography:** Using overlapping everyday objects (sport, shopping, coffee) instead of abstract icons makes the feature feel tangible and relatable without being literal.
- **Zero-state balance as trust signal:** Showing $0.00 balance prominently on first use normalizes the balance display and sets expectations for how the app tracks funds.

## Notes
- Hero card = onboarding + action collapsed into one — no separate walkthrough needed
- Peek right edge = cheapest possible scroll affordance, no indicator needed
- Yellow for money actions only — consistent semantic color role
- Floating dual CTAs above nav = always-on action shelf pattern
- White button inside colored card = avoids CTA color collision with page-level actions
- 3D loose illustration cluster feels warmer than flat icons — consider for fintech softening
- Balance card as "status at a glance" — logo mark doubles as account identifier

## Screen
Food discovery / recipe app home feed. Dark-mode first, content-forward layout. Sits at the top of the main discovery flow — the first thing a user sees after opening. Structure: logo + utility icons header → stories row → full-bleed hero card → horizontal filter chips → bottom nav.

## Layout & Spacing
Near edge-to-edge hero card dominates ~60% of screen height, creating immediate visual immersion. The card has subtle rounded corners and sits with tight horizontal margins (~16px each side), giving it a "floating" quality rather than full bleed. Stories row sits flush with minimal breathing room between header and cards. Filter chips float just above the nav bar, layered over the feed — implying they're persistent/sticky. Vertical rhythm feels intentional: header → stories → content → filters → nav, each zone clearly delineated without dividers.

## Components
- **Story circles**: Food photography thumbnails in circular frames with warm gold/amber ring borders — not the typical gradient ring, a single solid warm metallic tone. Slightly larger than standard story sizing.
- **Hero card**: Large rounded rectangle, full photo fill, text overlaid at bottom-left with gradient scrim. Heart icon top-right, minimal and white.
- **Category label**: Small all-caps text in warm amber/gold above the dish title — functions as a flavor/texture tag ("CRUNCHY").
- **Filter chips**: Pill-shaped, dark semi-transparent fill, emoji icon + label. Horizontally scrollable. Compact height.
- **Bottom nav**: Standard 5-tab, center tab elevated with white circle and sparkle icon — distinct CTA treatment.
- **"New drops" label**: Ghost text beneath center nav tab, adding contextual awareness to the action button.

## Typography & Color
- **Wordmark**: Chunky, bubbly custom lettering — playful, high personality
- **Category tag**: Small caps, amber/gold — creates a secondary hierarchy tier that feels editorial
- **Dish title**: Large, bold white serif-adjacent sans — high contrast against dark image
- **Chips**: Small, medium weight, white on dark

Color palette: Near-black background (#0a0a0a range), warm gold accents (story rings, category label), white for primary text and icons. Color used functionally: gold = discovery/curation signal, white = primary content, amber = attribute/mood tagging.

## Patterns
- **Dual discovery surfaces**: Stories row for quick browsing + hero card for deep-dive — two different engagement speeds on one screen
- **Attribute tagging above title**: The "CRUNCHY" label above the dish name is an editorial pattern borrowed from magazine design — communicates sensory/mood before the item name
- **Sticky filter chips above nav**: Filters float between content and nav rather than sitting in the header — keeps the top clean and puts filtering close to the thumb
- **"New drops" sub-label on nav CTA**: Adds urgency/novelty framing to what would otherwise be a generic action button — makes the center tab feel like a live, changing destination
- **Warm metallic story rings**: Departing from the ubiquitous gradient ring pattern; single-tone gold feels more premium and food-editorial
- **Emoji in filter chips**: Grounds abstract category names in immediate visual shorthand — faster scanning than text alone
- **Card floating with margins**: Slight inset from screen edges signals swipeability and that there's more content — implies card stack/feed without showing it

## Notes
- Gold accent as "editorial voice" color — not just brand, but signals curation
- "CRUNCHY" tag pattern → steal for any sensory/mood attribute system
- Sub-label under nav icon = great for surfacing freshness/urgency without badges
- Stories with food photography = works better than avatars for content-first apps
- Floating filter chips above nav = thumb-zone win, keeps header minimal
- Bubbly wordmark vs. clean body type = personality contrast that works
- Card inset margins as swipe affordance signal — don't go full bleed if you want to imply scrollability

## Screen
Personalized home/discovery screen for a sneaker/sportswear retail app. This is the logged-in home state — the first screen a returning user lands on. Layout flows vertically: top nav → personalized greeting → horizontal product carousel → editorial banner card → pagination dots → bottom nav.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Clean single-column vertical rhythm with generous white space between sections. The greeting sits high with notable breathing room above the product section. Product cards use a horizontal scroll carousel with visible card clipping on the right edge to signal scrollability. The editorial banner below uses a horizontal split layout (thumbnail left, text right). Consistent left-aligned text throughout. Section header uses a two-line stack (bold title + muted descriptor) paired with a right-aligned "View All" link.

---

## Components

- **Brand toggle (top left):** Two-logo pill switcher — both logos sit inside a rounded rectangle container, with the active brand appearing slightly more prominent. Subtle, elegant sub-brand switcher.
- **Search icon (top right):** Minimal ghost icon, no label.
- **Greeting header:** Large, warm personalized text — no container, just raw type.
- **Section header row:** Bold label + subdued descriptor stacked, with "View All" ghost text link right-aligned.
- **Product cards:** White background, rounded corners, large image-dominant cards. Product name in medium weight, category in light gray, price in regular weight below. No CTA button — tap the card.
- **Editorial banner card:** Rounded rectangle, light background, square thumbnail image left, text right. Emoji used inline as accent. Compact and content-dense.
- **Pagination dots:** Three dots below the banner, one filled/active, two unfilled — indicates swipeable content.
- **Bottom nav:** Five-item tab bar with icon + label. Profile tab has a small orange notification dot.

---

## Typography & Color

Near-monochromatic palette — white, light gray backgrounds, black text, mid-gray secondary text. The only color accent is the small orange dot on the profile tab icon, used purely as a notification signal. Type hierarchy: large bold greeting → medium bold section title → small gray descriptor → product name (medium) → category (light gray, smaller) → price (regular weight). Clean sans-serif throughout. Hierarchy is achieved through weight and gray value rather than color.

---

## Patterns

- **Time-aware greeting:** "Good Morning, Alex" — contextual personalization that makes the home screen feel alive and relational without being intrusive.
- **Sub-brand toggle as primary nav element:** Placing a brand-switcher at the very top left (where a hamburger or logo usually lives) is an unconventional choice that puts product line switching front and center — smart for a multi-line retailer.
- **Deliberate card clipping:** The third product card is intentionally half-cropped at the screen edge — a classic affordance signal for horizontal scroll, but here it's done with enough card visible to still be enticing.
- **Two-line section label:** "Top Picks for You" (bold) + "Recommended products" (muted) — the second line functions as a trust/context signal, explaining the personalization without UI chrome.
- **Editorial content as a card below commerce:** The "Sneakers of the Week" banner introduces a content/editorial layer beneath the transactional product row — blending editorial and commerce in a single scroll, keeping engagement high.
- **Emoji as micro-accent:** The ✨ emoji in the editorial card title adds warmth and informality without requiring custom illustration.
- **Notification dot placement:** Orange dot on the profile icon is the sole warm color on screen — immediately draws the eye without being aggressive.

---

## Notes
- Time-aware greetings → warmth without complexity
- Brand toggle in logo position = power move for multi-line apps
- Card clip = scroll affordance, don't hide it
- Two-line section headers: title + intent descriptor = trust layer
- Editorial card below product row = content-commerce blend, keeps scroll rewarding
- Emoji in UI copy = personality without illustration budget
- Single accent color (notification dot) = maximum signal, minimum noise
- All-gray palette makes product photography pop naturally

## Screen
Investment/savings portfolio home screen. Financial app dashboard showing total portfolio value, performance chart, and account breakdown. Entry point of the main app flow after authentication. Layout flows top-to-bottom: nav bar → hero balance → area chart → time filter → accounts list.

## Layout & Spacing
Balance figure sits flush left with generous top breathing room, creating a clear visual anchor. Chart bleeds edge-to-edge with no card container — it lives in open space. Time filter pills sit just below the chart baseline, tightly coupled to it. Accounts section begins with a section label + icon row, then stacked cards with consistent internal padding. Vertical rhythm is generous throughout — nothing feels cramped.

## Components
- **Balance display**: Oversized dollar figure (~36–40pt) with a small pill-shaped hide/reveal toggle button inline to the right — circular, light gray, eye-slash icon
- **Performance subtext**: Small muted label below balance showing all-time change
- **Area chart**: Full-width, no axes labels, no gridlines — minimal. Green stroke line with soft green-to-white gradient fill. Steps/plateaus visible suggesting discrete deposit events
- **Time range selector**: Text labels (1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y) as ghost options; "ALL" is selected — filled dark circle/pill, white text. High contrast active state
- **Account cards**: White rounded-rect cards with subtle shadow. Label left, value right. TFSA card has a sub-label ("2 accounts") and an expand chevron — indicating it's a grouped/collapsible item
- **Notification badge**: Red dot on the gift/rewards icon in the nav bar
- **Bottom nav**: 4 icons — home (filled/active), search, transfer, history

## Typography & Color
- Hero balance: Bold serif or heavy sans, very large — commands immediate attention
- Section labels ("Accounts"): Medium weight, moderate size
- Card labels: Regular weight, comfortable reading size
- Sub-labels: Small, muted gray — clear tertiary hierarchy
- **Palette**: White background, near-black text, muted gray for secondary text, sage/olive green for chart (not a saturated green — feels calm and premium), dark charcoal for selected pill and active nav icon, red dot for notification only

## Patterns
- **Inline balance privacy toggle**: The hide button is placed directly adjacent to the number rather than in settings — immediate, contextual, low friction for public use
- **Chartless chart**: No axes, no labels, no gridlines — the chart communicates trend/shape only, not precise values. Reduces cognitive load, keeps it emotional rather than analytical
- **Step-function chart shape**: Visually communicates deposits as discrete events — honest representation of how a savings account actually grows
- **Grouped account expansion**: TFSA shows "2 accounts" sub-label with a chevron — the card itself is a parent container, implying expandable sub-rows below. Partially visible content at scroll bottom reinforces there's more
- **Time filter as floating selector over chart**: The filter row is visually attached to the chart, not separated into its own section — makes the relationship between control and output spatially obvious
- **Gift/rewards icon with badge in nav bar**: Surfaces a promotional or referral feature at the top-right without disrupting the primary nav — keeps it discoverable but not intrusive

## Notes
- Hide-balance toggle inline with number = great for shoulder surfing situations, steal this
- Area chart with zero axis labels = trust the shape, not the numbers — works for savings, risky for investing
- Step chart shape = honest data viz, not smoothed vanity curves
- "2 accounts" sub-label on card = metadata without opening the item
- Partial card visible at bottom = scroll affordance without explicit indicator
- Muted green palette = money without aggression — warm, not clinical
- Red badge only on gift icon = single notification color anchor, nothing competing

## Screen
Dating/social discovery app — profile card view within the main swipe/browse feed. This is the core matching screen where users evaluate potential connections. Full-bleed card layout with profile photo dominating the upper ~75% of the screen, identity info overlaid at the bottom of the card, with action buttons anchored to the card's lower corners.

## Layout & Spacing
Card takes edge-to-edge width with slight top margin from the nav. Profile info sits in a gradient-overlay zone at the card bottom — name/age on one line, two metadata rows below with icon+text pairs. Action buttons (heart, star) float over the card corners at bottom, creating an asymmetric but balanced composition. Bottom nav sits cleanly below the card with consistent icon+label treatment.

## Components
- **Profile card**: Full-bleed photo with soft bottom gradient scrim enabling white text legibility without a hard overlay
- **Name/age badge**: Bold white text with a small verification/badge icon inline — tight kerning, high contrast
- **Metadata rows**: Icon + text pairs (briefcase, graduation cap) in white, secondary weight — iconography carries semantic meaning quickly
- **Action buttons (FAB-style)**: Two circular buttons — small yellow heart (bottom-left), large yellow star (bottom-right) — size hierarchy signals primary vs secondary action
- **Share button**: Small frosted/dark circular icon top-right of card — unobtrusive utility action
- **Nav badges**: "50+" and "1" notification badges on nav items — pill-shaped with brand yellow and orange respectively, color-coded by notification type

## Typography & Color
- Name is the largest, boldest text — clear entry point
- Age follows inline, same weight, slightly smaller feel
- Metadata is regular weight, smaller — clear 3-level hierarchy
- **Palette**: Warm yellow (#F9C000 range) as primary brand accent used on both action buttons and nav badges; white for all card overlay text; near-black for nav labels; orange used distinctly for chat badge — functional color differentiation between notification types

## Patterns
- **Asymmetric FAB sizing**: The "super like" star button is noticeably larger than the heart button — size itself communicates action weight/cost without any label
- **Color-coded notification badges**: Yellow for likes (social proof, positive), orange for messages (urgent/action-required) — two different emotional registers communicated through hue alone
- **Inline credential iconography**: Job and education shown with universally understood icons rather than labels ("Job:" / "School:") — saves horizontal space and scans faster
- **Photo-as-context storytelling**: The profile photo is taken in front of a large advertisement poster of a smiling man — creates an unintentional but interesting layered visual that adds personality/humor, suggesting the app doesn't over-curate photo presentation
- **Badge overflow truncation**: "50+" rather than exact number — reduces anxiety of showing a large precise count while still communicating high social proof
- **Verification badge inline with name**: Trust signal placed at the most-read element (name line) rather than buried elsewhere

## Notes
- FAB size = action importance — no labels needed
- Yellow = positive/social, orange = urgent/response — worth stealing for notification systems
- Scrim gradient on photo cards: let the image breathe, don't box the text
- "50+" truncation pattern: social proof without intimidating specificity
- Inline icon metadata > labeled fields for quick-scan profiles
- Verification mark lives on the name, not in a separate section — trust where eyes go first

## Screen
Personalized home/dashboard screen for a book summary / self-improvement app. This is the post-onboarding "For You" feed — the main landing screen after login. Layout is a vertical scroll with a fixed bottom nav and a sticky-ish stats card at top, followed by horizontal scroll sections.

---

## Layout & Spacing

Top-to-bottom hierarchy: logo header → stats card → CTA banner → category chips (horizontal scroll) → content section header + subtitle → book card carousel. Generous section padding (~24px) separates each content block. The stats card uses an internal two-column split (streak left, weekly metrics right) with a blue CTA banner flush to the card's bottom edge — the banner feels "attached" to the card rather than floating separately. Book cards are tall and nearly square, creating a visually rich carousel.

---

## Components

- **Stats card**: Rounded card with two zones — left shows streak (icon + large number + label), right shows three colored metrics in a row. Bottom edge has a full-width blue pill/banner for daily mission CTA with chevron.
- **Daily Mission banner**: Solid blue, all-caps small text, right-aligned chevron. Feels like a tab or footer of the card above.
- **Category chips**: Outlined rounded rectangles with illustrated emoji-style icons left of label text. Partially cropped third chip signals horizontal scrollability.
- **Book summary cards**: Large illustrated cover cards with bold typographic treatments directly on the cover art. No separate title below — the title is baked into the illustration. Tall aspect ratio (~3:4).
- **"Roll the dice" row**: Floating bottom sheet-style strip with dice emoji icon, bold label, and subdued descriptor. Acts as a persistent discovery shortcut above the nav bar.
- **Bottom nav**: Standard 4-tab bar; active tab has filled colored icon + colored label. Profile tab has an orange notification dot.

---

## Typography & Color

**Type hierarchy**: Section headers are bold ~20px; subsection subtitles are regular ~14px gray; stat numbers are large bold (~32px) in accent colors; card titles are display-weight, all-caps, baked into artwork. Clean sans-serif throughout.

**Color use**:
- Blue (#3B6EF8 approx) = primary action (daily mission banner, active nav)
- Purple/violet = key points metric + category icons
- Green = minutes metric
- Orange = insights metric + streak flame icon
- Color-coded stats make each metric instantly distinguishable without labels at a glance
- Card backgrounds are full-bleed illustrated color (yellow, dark green) — no neutral cards in the content feed

---

## Patterns

- **Metric colorization as identity**: Each weekly stat gets its own distinct color — not just for decoration but as a quick-scan encoding system. Users learn "green = time" without reading.
- **CTA anchored to data card**: The "Your Daily Mission" button is visually fused to the stats card, implying causality — your stats lead to your mission. Clever grouping.
- **Illustrated covers as content cards**: Rather than text-list summaries, book cards are treated as visual artifacts with bold editorial typography. Raises perceived content quality dramatically.
- **"Roll the dice" persistent strip**: Serendipity feature surfaced as a persistent element just above the nav — not buried in Explore. Lowers decision fatigue and encourages exploration without requiring navigation.
- **Horizontal scroll with deliberate crop**: Category chips and book cards both show a partial next item — a well-executed affordance that signals scrollability without any indicator dots or arrows.
- **Notification dot on Profile tab**: Orange dot creates tension/curiosity without being a red badge alarm — softer urgency.
- **Personalization framing in headers**: "Categories you're interested in" and "To get you started / Popular summaries we picked just for you" — copy reinforces algorithmic curation as personal service, not automation.

---

## Notes

- Stats card + CTA fused = one unit, not two — try this for dashboard + action combos
- Color-per-metric system > icon-per-metric for fast scanning
- Book covers as full-bleed art cards → elevates content, no thumbnail grids
- "Roll the dice" = serendipity as a persistent UI element, not a feature buried in menus
- Partial crop on horizontal scroll = implicit affordance, no dots needed
- Warm/playful illustrated icons in category chips keep it light, not corporate
- Orange notif dot softer than red — consider urgency spectrum in badge colors
- Section subtitle ("we picked just for you") does personalization work copy can do

## Screen
Mental wellness / self-improvement app. This is a content preview sheet that appears mid-journey flow — a bottom sheet modal overlaying an active lesson or exercise screen. The user has tapped into a practice module and is seeing a preview card before committing to start. Layout is: persistent background screen (dimmed) + floating card with upward-pointing notch/arrow connecting it to the triggering element above.

## Layout & Spacing
Card uses generous internal padding (~20-24px sides). Content stacks vertically: metadata row → illustration banner → headline → body copy → sources card → CTA button. The illustration banner bleeds edge-to-edge within the card, creating a full-width visual break. The sources section is inset with its own card-within-card treatment, adding a secondary containment layer. Comfortable breathing room between body copy and sources (~24px gap).

## Components
- **Bottom sheet / modal card** — white, rounded corners (~16px), with a triangular upward notch at the top edge pointing to the originating UI element. Unusual connector detail.
- **Illustration banner** — light blue rounded rectangle, full card width, featuring a centered character illustration. Soft, flat art style.
- **Metadata label** — "Practice • 4 min" in small gray text, paired with an X dismiss icon top-right. Minimal, unobtrusive.
- **Sources sub-card** — outlined/bordered card with a "Sources" label in muted gray, article title in medium weight, byline in small gray, and a "See all" link right-aligned. Credibility signal embedded naturally.
- **CTA button** — full-width, solid purple/violet, rounded pill shape. No label visible (likely cut off or intentionally minimal).
- **Progress indicator** — thin bar at ~18% fill in the top dropdown, showing course completion state.
- **Streak counter** — flame icon + number, top nav area.

## Typography & Color
- **Headline**: Large, bold, dark near-black — clear primary hierarchy
- **Body**: Medium-small, medium gray, regular weight — readable secondary level
- **Metadata/labels**: Small, muted gray — tertiary, low visual noise
- **Sources title**: Medium weight dark, with light gray supporting text below
- Color palette: Deep teal/muted green background, white card, light blue illustration bg, violet/purple as primary action color, warm orange for streak icon. Purple used exclusively for active states and CTA — strong signal value.

## Patterns
- **Arrow-connected modal**: The upward-pointing notch on the card visually anchors the sheet to its trigger point on screen — like a tooltip-meets-bottom-sheet hybrid. Rare and spatially intelligent.
- **Credibility scaffolding**: Embedding a cited source card directly in a practice preview is non-standard — it pre-answers "why should I trust this?" before the user even starts.
- **Time commitment upfront**: "4 min" in the header sets expectation before any scrolling — reduces friction and abandonment.
- **Illustration as emotional priming**: The character holding a shield/badge visually communicates the exercise theme (self-protection, wins) before any text is read.
- **Background context bleed**: The underlying screen remains partially visible and readable, maintaining spatial orientation — user never loses their place.
- **Streak mechanic in nav**: Flame + number placed in top bar rather than a separate screen keeps motivation ambient rather than intrusive.

## Notes
- Arrow notch on bottom sheet = tooltip DNA + sheet DNA hybrid — steal this for contextual overlays
- Sources card = trust signal without leaving the flow, great for edu/wellness apps
- Time-to-complete as metadata label pattern — always include for exercises/lessons
- Illustration banner inside card (not full bleed on screen) keeps it contained and skimmable
- Purple single-color CTA against white card = maximum contrast, zero ambiguity
- Consider: what if all modals showed where they "came from" spatially?
- Progress % in dropdown selector = compact but always-visible progress — no separate progress screen needed

## Screen
Health/wellness tracking app — the main Summary/dashboard screen. This is the home tab, the first screen users land on. Layout is a vertically scrolling feed with a large header, followed by pinned metric cards, a navigation row, and promotional/onboarding content below. Primary interactions: tapping metric cards to drill into detail, editing pinned items, avatar for profile access.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Warm gradient header bleeds edge-to-edge behind the title, creating a distinct "above the fold" zone before white card content begins. Cards are full-width with generous internal padding and large corner radii (~16–20px). Vertical rhythm between cards is consistent (~12–16px gaps). The "Pinned" section label + Edit link use a tight label/action pairing at the same baseline. Content sections are chunked: Pinned → Navigation row → Promotional — each with clear visual separation.

---

## Components
- **Gradient header band** — warm peach-to-orange gradient, fades into the page background; contains large bold title + avatar
- **Avatar/initials chip** — muted lavender circle with white initials; tappable profile entry point
- **Metric cards** — white, heavily rounded, shadow-less; contain icon + label header, date + chevron, large numeric value with unit label, and a mini bar chart
- **Empty state card** — same card shell as data card but shows "No Data" in muted gray — consistent container regardless of data state
- **"Show All" navigation row** — standalone card with app icon thumbnail + label + chevron; acts as a persistent escape hatch
- **Promotional/feature card** — illustration icon (blue, outlined style) + headline + body copy + dismissible X; partially clipped at bottom to signal scrollability
- **Bottom tab bar** — frosted/pill-shaped background, selected state uses filled icon + colored label

---

## Typography & Color
- **Title**: Very large, heavy weight, near-black — establishes strong hierarchy immediately
- **Section labels** ("Pinned", "Get More From Health"): Bold, medium size, black
- **Edit link**: Blue, same size as section label — standard iOS tappable text
- **Metric value**: Large numerals, bold, with a smaller regular-weight unit suffix inline — classic big-number pattern
- **"No Data"**: Medium gray, medium weight — communicates absence without alarm
- **Color palette**: Warm peach/orange gradient (header), white (cards), red-orange accent (active bar in chart + Steps icon), muted lavender (avatar), blue (icon accents, links, selected nav), gray (empty states, inactive bars)
- Color is used functionally: red-orange = activity/energy, blue = navigation/interaction, gray = inactive/empty

---

## Patterns
- **Gradient header as mood-setter**: The warm gradient doesn't carry data — it sets emotional tone and visually anchors the page title before transitioning to neutral white content. Rare and effective.
- **Consistent card shell for empty states**: "No Data" uses the identical card structure as populated cards — avoids layout shift and teaches the user the pattern even before data exists.
- **Inline mini-chart within card**: The bar chart is embedded directly in the metric card rather than on a detail screen — gives just enough data scent to motivate a tap without overwhelming.
- **Big number + small unit suffix**: "2,114 steps" — number is display-size, unit is body-size inline. Lets the eye grab the number first, then context.
- **Dismissible promotional card with clipping**: The feature card is intentionally cut off at the scroll boundary — a scroll affordance signal that doubles as content teasing.
- **Avatar as profile entry**: Initials-based avatar in the header corner is a clean, low-footprint way to surface account access without a dedicated nav item.
- **Edit affordance scoped to section**: "Edit" appears only next to "Pinned," not globally — reduces cognitive scope of what editing means.

---

## Notes
- Warm gradient header → instant personality without custom illustration
- Empty state = same card shell, just swap content → no layout jank
- Big number / small unit inline → steal this always
- Clip promo card at fold = scroll invitation
- Avatar initials chip in header corner = lightweight profile access pattern
- Mini sparkline/bar chart in card = data scent without commitment
- Section-scoped edit link > global edit mode for simple lists
- Peach/orange for vitality, blue for interaction — clear semantic color split

## Screen
A running/fitness app's primary "Start Run" screen. This is the main action hub — the screen a user lands on when they're ready to begin a workout. Layout flows top-to-bottom: header with avatar + bookmark, large page title, segmented tab switcher, contextual info card carousel, full-width map view, and a floating action cluster anchored above the bottom nav.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Content lives in a clean white zone at top, then transitions into a full-bleed map that takes up roughly 45% of the screen. The map acts as a spatial canvas for the action layer. The START button and flanking utility buttons float over the map, creating a layered z-axis composition. The "Set a Goal" text sits as a quiet secondary action just below the primary CTA cluster. Generous padding on the header section; the card carousel bleeds off the right edge signaling horizontal scroll.

---

## Components
- **Avatar (top-left):** Small circular photo, no border, acts as profile entry point
- **Bookmark icon (top-right):** Minimal stroke icon, lightweight utility
- **Tab switcher:** Underline-style, no pill/background — active state is bold black, inactive is gray. Very low visual weight
- **Info card:** Rounded rectangle, white card with icon block on left (light gray square bg), title + subtitle text. Carousel format, partially visible next card teases scrollability
- **Map:** Desaturated/low-contrast street map used as texture/background, not primary UI. Blue dot = user location
- **Shoe pin:** Product image in circular white container on map — contextual gear callout
- **Radio/signal pin:** White circle with broadcast icon — likely nearby runners or live activity
- **START button:** Large filled orange circle, bold italic all-caps label. Dominant CTA. Size is dramatically larger than surrounding elements
- **Flanking icon buttons:** Two small white rounded-square buttons (settings + music) symmetrically placed left/right of START
- **"Set a Goal" button:** Pill-shaped, white background, thin border — ghost-style secondary action
- **Bottom nav:** Standard 5-tab, icon + label, active state uses filled/colored icon

---

## Typography & Color
- **Title "Run":** Large, heavy serif-adjacent weight, black — establishes section identity
- **Tab labels:** Medium weight, size differential between active/inactive only (no color change, just weight + underline)
- **Card title:** Medium-large, semi-bold; subtitle is smaller, gray — classic two-level hierarchy
- **START label:** Bold italic, white on orange — energy and urgency conveyed through type style, not just color
- **Color palette:** Near-white background, black type, mid-gray for secondary text/icons, orange as the single accent color for the primary action. Blue dot for GPS location. Extremely restrained — orange carries all the energy.

---

## Patterns
- **Map-as-canvas CTA layer:** Rather than a modal or separate screen, the START action floats directly over the map. The map provides context (you're about to run *here*) while the action layer stays accessible. Clever spatial storytelling.
- **Asymmetric button sizing as hierarchy:** The START button is 3–4x the diameter of flanking buttons. No ambiguity about primary action. The size difference alone communicates priority without color alone doing the work.
- **Contextual pre-run intel via card carousel:** UV index, weather-style advisories surfaced before the run starts — useful, non-intrusive, swipeable. Treats the runner as someone who needs environmental context, not just a timer.
- **Product pin on map:** Embedding a gear/shoe callout as a map marker is a subtle commerce integration that feels native to the experience rather than interruptive.
- **Secondary CTA placement:** "Set a Goal" sits *below* START, visually subordinate but spatially close — accessible without competing. Respects the hierarchy.
- **Tab switcher with zero visual noise:** No pills, no backgrounds — just weight and underline. Keeps the header zone extremely clean.
- **Italic type on CTA:** Using italic weight on "START" adds kinetic energy — implies motion before the run even begins. Typographic personality as UX signal.

---

## Notes
- Orange as single accent = high signal-to-noise, every orange element is interactive/important
- Map bleed under action buttons = depth without modals
- Try italic bold type on motion-related CTAs — communicates action kinetically
- Shoe/product as map pin = commerce that doesn't feel like commerce
- Pre-activity contextual cards (weather, UV) = great pattern for any activity-based app
- Flanking utility buttons symmetrically placed = visual balance without stealing focus from center CTA
- Ghost pill for secondary action below primary = clean way to offer an alternative without competing

## Screen
E-commerce store management dashboard — the home/overview screen for a merchant after initial setup. Sits in the post-onboarding phase, showing setup progress and upsell prompts. Layout is a single scrollable column with a sticky header, search bar, promotional banner, checklist card, and upsell card partially visible at bottom.

## Layout & Spacing
Content divided into clear horizontal bands: header → search → promo strip → setup checklist card → upsell card. The checklist items live inside a rounded card with generous internal padding, creating visual containment. Consistent left-alignment of text with icon+label+chevron row pattern. The partial visibility of the bottom upsell card signals scrollability without explicit affordance.

## Components
- **Avatar/monogram button** — Purple rounded square with white initials, top-left nav anchor
- **Notification bell** — Outline icon, top-right
- **Search bar** — Full-width pill/rounded rect, light gray fill, search icon + placeholder "Go to..." — acts as command palette hint, not just search
- **Promo banner strip** — Inline text row with gold sparkle icon, no background card, very minimal
- **Progress badge** — Small outlined pill tag "5 / 6 completed" — subtle, low-weight border, sits below section header
- **Checklist rows** — Icon + label + chevron. Completed items: dark filled circle with white checkmark. Incomplete: dashed circle outline. All rows same height, divider-free separation via spacing alone
- **Upsell card** — White rounded card, large gold sparkle cluster illustration, bold headline, subtext, CTA button partially cropped

## Typography & Color
- Section header ("Get ready to sell") — bold, ~18–20px, near-black
- Subtext — regular weight, ~14px, medium gray
- Checklist labels — medium weight, ~15px, near-black; completed items retain same weight (no strikethrough — intentional)
- Progress badge — small, gray, low contrast
- Color palette: near-white background (#F5F5F5 range), white cards, near-black text, medium gray secondary text, purple for avatar, gold/amber for sparkle accents and promo elements
- Gold sparkle used exclusively for promotional/upsell moments — creates a consistent "reward/upgrade" color signal

## Patterns
- **"Go to..." command palette framing** — Search bar labeled "Go to..." implies navigation/command intent rather than content search — power-user pattern embedded in a consumer-feeling UI
- **Dashed circle for incomplete vs. solid check for complete** — Visual weight difference is stark and immediate; no color needed to distinguish state
- **No strikethrough on completed tasks** — Keeps list readable and non-cluttered; completion communicated purely through icon swap
- **Partial card crop at scroll boundary** — Upsell card intentionally bleeds off screen to communicate more content below without a "scroll down" label
- **Inline promo strip before the card** — Lightweight single-line promotional message above the fold, before the heavier upsell card below — two-tier upsell hierarchy (soft then hard)
- **Progress as a badge, not a progress bar** — "5 / 6 completed" as a pill tag is quieter than a bar, reducing anxiety while still communicating momentum
- **Sparkle icon as upsell visual language** — Consistent gold sparkle motif ties the inline promo strip to the bottom upsell card, creating visual rhyme across scroll distance

## Notes
- "Go to..." > "Search" — reframes search as navigation, feels more powerful
- Dashed circle = pending / filled check = done — no color dependency, accessible
- No strikethrough on done tasks — cleaner, more respectful UX
- Sparkle = upgrade/money signal — use consistently as a system-wide upsell marker
- Soft promo strip + hard upsell card = two-step sell, not one loud banner
- Progress pill badge instead of bar — less pressure, same info
- Partial bottom card crop = implicit scroll cue, no label needed
- Avatar as nav entry point top-left — doubles as account access + personalization signal

## Screen
Travel booking app home/dashboard screen. Entry point after login — serves as a navigation hub to all booking categories plus personalized content. Layout flows top-to-bottom: greeting header → category grid → loyalty status card → editorial content strip.

## Layout & Spacing
Content organized in clear horizontal bands with generous white space between sections. The 3×2 category grid uses equal-weight cells with consistent padding. The loyalty banner sits as a full-width card between functional navigation and inspirational content — a deliberate placement separating utility from discovery. The bottom photo strip bleeds off-screen to signal horizontal scrollability. Consistent left-aligned section headers anchor each content zone.

## Components
- **Category grid tiles**: White rounded-rect cards with illustrated icons (not flat — slight depth/color), label below icon, subtle border/shadow. 3-column top row, 3-column bottom row.
- **Loyalty status badge**: Pill-shaped button (dark navy fill, white text + snowflake-style icon) in top-right of greeting row — compact but prominent.
- **Loyalty benefit card**: Full-width card with left-aligned icon cluster, bold body copy, and a standalone blue text link CTA. No button — link only, keeps it low-pressure.
- **Photo cards**: Large editorial image cards with text label overlaid at bottom-left on dark gradient. Partially cropped third card signals carousel behavior.
- **Chat FAB**: Circular white button with chat icon, floating over photo content area.

## Typography & Color
- **Greeting**: Large, bold sans-serif — high visual weight, personal
- **Category labels**: Small, regular weight, centered below icon
- **Loyalty card copy**: Medium body, bold key phrase ("Blue"), blue hyperlink for CTA
- **Section header**: Medium-large, bold, left-aligned
- **Photo labels**: White, bold, overlaid on imagery

Color palette: Navy/dark blue as primary brand anchor. Yellow accent used sparingly (logo mark). Blue used for interactive links and active nav state. Cards are white on light gray background — low contrast container approach. Photography provides all warmth and color variety.

## Patterns
- **Personalized greeting + status in one row**: Name on left, loyalty tier badge on right — communicates identity and status simultaneously without a dedicated profile section
- **Loyalty nudge positioned between utility and inspiration**: The benefit card isn't at the top (would feel pushy) or bottom (would be ignored) — it bridges the functional grid and the aspirational content, contextually appropriate
- **First-name personalization in loyalty card copy**: "Alex, you'll save..." — repeating the name inside a card creates a second moment of personalization, reinforcing relevance
- **Editorial section framing**: "Go beyond your typical stay" — aspirational copy reframes what could be a generic category list into a discovery prompt, shifting user mindset from transactional to exploratory
- **Illustrated icons with color depth**: Icons aren't flat monochrome — they use a limited palette with slight dimensionality, making the grid feel warmer and more premium than typical utility nav
- **Partial card bleed**: Third photo card is intentionally cropped to communicate scrollability without any explicit affordance like dots or arrows
- **Low-pressure loyalty CTA**: Text link instead of button keeps the loyalty card informational rather than salesy

## Notes
- Loyalty tier as header-row badge = elegant — doesn't interrupt flow, always visible
- 3×2 grid sweet spot for travel: covers all major verticals without overwhelming
- Benefit card = "you're already winning" framing, not upsell — smart retention tone
- Editorial section copy does heavy lifting — same cards with "More categories" would feel flat
- Illustrated icons > flat icons for warmth in aspirational product categories
- FAB over content = acceptable when content is browsing-mode, not task-mode
- Consider: does the chat FAB compete visually with the partially-visible third card? Placement tension worth noting

## Screen
AI-powered search/research app mid-query state. This is the "thinking" or "loading" screen shown while an AI agent actively browses the web to answer a user's question. Sits between query submission and results delivery. Layout is minimal — query at top, processing status in the lower half, persistent toolbar at bottom.

## Layout & Spacing
Content is split into two loose zones: the query display anchored near the top with generous empty space below it (implying the answer is being generated into that void), and a status block in the lower third showing sources being read. The empty middle space is intentional — it creates anticipation and breathing room rather than filling with a spinner. Source list uses tight line-height with no separators, feeling like a live stream of data.

## Components
- **Query text** — bold, purple, left-aligned, large size. Treated as a heading/title, not an input field in this state.
- **Status label** ("Reading 20 web pages") — bold purple, slightly smaller, acts as a live activity headline.
- **Source list** — muted lavender/gray text, smaller size, stacked with minimal spacing. Truncated with "… and more" to suggest depth without overwhelming.
- **Bottom gradient fade** — deep purple gradient sweeping up from the toolbar area, visually anchoring the AI toggle and creating a dramatic stage for the mode switcher.
- **AI/Search toggle pill** — rounded pill with two icons (sparkle/AI and a search engine logo). Active state (AI) sits inside a white elevated circle, inactive is flat. Floats over the gradient.
- **Bottom toolbar** — light lavender bar with three items: a thumbnail of a prior result (left), a centered "+" add button (pill-shaped, gray), and an up-arrow button (right).

## Typography & Color
- **Type hierarchy**: Query > Status label > Source URLs > "…and more" — each step down reduces weight and shifts toward muted lavender.
- **Color palette**: White background, vivid purple (#5B2ECC range) for key text and gradient, soft lavender for secondary text and toolbar background, white for elevated toggle state.
- **Color function**: Purple = active/AI/intelligence. Lavender = supporting/passive. White = selected/active state within toggle. The gradient acts as a color signal — you're in AI mode, not standard search.

## Patterns
- **Purposeful empty space as anticipation** — the large white gap between query and sources isn't a mistake; it's a canvas where the answer will appear, making the loading state feel generative rather than blocked.
- **Live source transparency** — showing the actual URLs being read builds trust and mimics the feeling of watching someone research in real time. The "… and more" truncation implies thoroughness without visual clutter.
- **Gradient as mode indicator** — the purple gradient at the bottom isn't decorative; it signals which mode is active (AI vs. standard search) through environmental color rather than just the toggle state alone.
- **Toggle as persistent mode switcher** — the AI/search toggle floats over the gradient, always accessible mid-process. Lets users bail to standard search without navigating away.
- **Thumbnail in toolbar** — small preview of a prior result/tab in the bottom left gives spatial context and quick access without a full tab switcher.
- **Status as narrative** — "Reading 20 web pages" frames the AI's work as a story beat, not just a progress indicator. Verb-first phrasing makes it feel alive.

## Notes
- Empty space = anticipation, not emptiness — use intentionally in loading states
- Source list as trust signal — show your work, truncate gracefully
- Gradient as mode color-coding — environment changes, not just UI element
- "Reading X sources" > generic spinner — specificity = credibility
- Floating toggle over gradient is clever: mode switch always reachable, visually tied to the mode color
- Toolbar thumbnail = lightweight tab history, no full tab UI needed
- Purple bold for query echoes back user's own words as the "headline" of the moment

## Screen
Explore/discovery screen from a health & fasting app. Sits as a secondary destination tab (not home), serving as a content hub for articles, challenges, and curated material. Layout is a vertically scrolling feed organized into named horizontal sections, with a segmented sub-navigation at the top.

## Layout & Spacing
Three-tier hierarchy: top nav bar → sub-tab strip → scrollable content sections. Each section uses a bold left-aligned section label with generous top margin before content begins. Featured hero card spans full width. Challenge cards use a horizontal scroll carousel with a peek pattern (third card partially visible). Content sections stack vertically with consistent inter-section breathing room (~24–32px). Left/right margins are uniform throughout (~16px).

## Components
- **Sub-tab strip**: Three text tabs (HOME, LEARN, CHALLENGES) in all-caps small type; active state uses a soft pink/salmon pill background with colored text, inactive tabs are plain gray — subtle but clear
- **Featured hero card**: Split two-column layout — left panel deep blue with label + headline + CTA button, right panel lighter blue with illustrated graphic. Rounded corners (~16px). CTA is a dark maroon/burgundy pill button, high contrast against blue
- **Challenge cards**: White cards with rounded corners, drop shadow. Top icon is a custom badge/illustration. Category label in small green all-caps, bold title, metadata line in muted gray (dates or participant count)
- **Horizontal carousel**: Cards are uniform width, peek pattern reveals next item to signal scrollability
- **Section header row**: Left-weighted title + right-aligned "SEE ALL" in small red/coral caps — classic but well-executed

## Typography & Color
- Section headers: Large bold (~20px), near-black
- Card titles: Bold, medium-large (~18px), near-black
- Category labels: All-caps, small (~11px), green — used as content-type signifier
- Metadata: Small, light gray — clearly tertiary
- Color palette: Deep royal blue, light sky blue, dark maroon/burgundy, coral/salmon pink, green (badge), white cards on light gray background
- Color is used functionally: blue = featured/editorial, green = active/fasting category, coral = active nav state, maroon = primary CTA

## Patterns
- **Split-panel hero card**: Dividing a card into two tonal zones (dark blue / light blue) creates visual interest without an image, while keeping the illustration as a decorative element rather than background noise
- **Content-type labeling before title**: "ARTICLE" as a micro-label above the headline sets expectation before reading — reduces cognitive load on what you're about to tap into
- **Peek carousel as implicit affordance**: Partially cropped third card is a clean scroll invitation without any explicit "swipe" instruction
- **Social proof in card metadata**: "456.3k active" on challenge cards is embedded directly into the card body — not a badge overlay — making participation data feel informational rather than promotional
- **Maroon CTA on blue**: Unexpected color pairing for a CTA button that avoids the typical white-on-blue; creates strong contrast while feeling premium
- **Sub-tabs within a tab**: Using a secondary tab strip inside an already-tabbed screen to subdivide content types — keeps the top-level nav clean while allowing deep content categorization

## Notes
- Split-panel card = great for editorial content without photography dependency
- All-caps green category label = reusable content-type system, easy to extend
- Maroon pill button on blue bg — save this combo
- "456k active" social proof inline in card body, not as a badge — feels editorial not gamified
- Peek pattern + uniform card width = safe horizontal scroll pattern
- Sub-tab pill active state: soft background fill, no underline — feels modern vs traditional tab underline
- Section label + SEE ALL on same row = standard but the coral/red SEE ALL color ties back to brand accent nicely

## Screen
Fitness/workout app home screen — the pre-workout launch screen where users select and start a workout session. Sits at the top of the core task flow. Layout is vertically stacked: top nav chip → large hero illustration zone → greeting + contextual info → floating mascot character → workout selector bar → primary CTA → bottom nav.

## Layout & Spacing
Generous empty space in the upper two-thirds creates a "stage" effect for the animated hero icon. Content clusters tightly at the bottom third, creating a strong visual weight drop. The workout selector and start button are grouped as a distinct bottom sheet-like zone without a card container — they float on the dark background. The mascot character deliberately bleeds upward out of the selector bar, breaking the grid in an intentional, playful way.

## Components
- **Activity chip (top left):** Small pill/capsule with icon + label on dark gray background — functions as current activity context or quick-switch selector
- **Hero activity icon:** Large, glowing cyan/teal illustrated figure on treadmill — not a flat icon, has subtle gradient/glow treatment suggesting animation capability
- **Greeting text:** Large bold white headline, centered
- **Weather strip:** Monospaced-style small text with inline emoji icons for temperature and condition — feels like a status bar element embedded in content
- **3D mascot character:** Rendered toy/figurine character overlapping the selector bar — purely delightful, non-functional
- **Workout selector bar:** Dark pill-shaped container with activity icon + label on left, search icon center-right, and a separate deep red pill button on the right for duration ("25min >")
- **Start button:** Full-width white rounded rectangle with bold black label — high contrast CTA
- **Bottom nav:** Three icons (profile, add/plus, stats) minimal, icon-only

## Typography & Color
- Headline: Large, heavy weight, white — warm and conversational tone ("Great to see you!")
- Weather info: Small, monospaced or condensed font, muted white/gray — clearly secondary
- Selector label: Medium weight, white on dark
- Duration button: White text on deep crimson/burgundy red pill
- Start button: Bold black on white — maximum contrast

**Palette:** Near-black background (#0a0a0a), white text, cyan/teal hero glow, deep red accent for duration selector, gray for secondary elements. Color used functionally: red = configurable/adjustable parameter, white = primary action, cyan = activity identity.

## Patterns
- **Mascot as transition element:** The 3D character sits precisely at the boundary between content and action zones — acts as a visual bridge and adds personality without cluttering functional UI
- **Split pill CTA bar:** Combining workout type selector + search + duration into one compound pill component is clever — three functions, one visual unit, low cognitive load
- **Weather as workout context:** Surfacing ambient conditions on a workout start screen is genuinely useful (outdoor vs. indoor decision) and adds smart/aware product feel without requiring a separate screen
- **Glow treatment on hero icon:** The cyan glow on the activity illustration implies interactivity/selection state — communicates "this is your chosen activity" without a label saying so
- **Empty stage space:** The vast empty center isn't wasted — it's breathing room that makes the app feel premium and focused, not data-dense
- **Monospaced weather text:** Using a different type style for the weather line creates a "system readout" feel that subtly differentiates it from UI copy

## Notes
- Split-function pill bar worth stealing — type + search + parameter in one row
- 3D mascot overlapping UI layer = personality without real estate cost
- Weather context on workout home = smart ambient data injection
- Glow icon as selection state indicator — no checkmark needed
- "Stage" layout: pack everything bottom 35%, let top breathe
- Deep red for adjustable params vs. white for commit action — good color grammar
- Monospaced secondary info = feels like live data, not static copy
- Greeting copy matters — conversational tone sets emotional register immediately

## Screen
Crypto exchange home/dashboard screen. This is the primary logged-in landing screen of a trading app, sitting at the top of the main navigation hierarchy. Layout is a single-column vertical scroll with a persistent top nav, segmented tab switcher, portfolio summary hero area, and a modular widget grid below.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Content is organized in distinct horizontal bands: nav bar → search → portfolio value hero → earn promo card → dismissible banner → 2-column widget grid → secondary tab bar → bottom nav. The 2-column widget grid breaks the otherwise full-width rhythm, creating visual density contrast. Generous padding (~16–20px) within cards keeps content breathable despite information density. The hero value section uses asymmetric layout — large number left, CTA button right — creating strong visual tension.

---

## Components

- **Segmented toggle (Exchange/Wallet):** Pill-style, unbordered, with active state shown via heavier font weight rather than background fill — subtle but effective
- **Notification badge:** Yellow dot with count on message icon — high contrast against dark icon
- **Portfolio value display:** Oversized monospace-style number, fiat equivalent in muted smaller text below, PNL row in red with chevron for expansion
- **Add Funds CTA:** Yellow filled rounded rectangle button — primary action, floated right of value display
- **Earn card:** White card with icon, headline, and secondary yellow outlined "Subscribe" button — contained promotional unit
- **Dismissible banner:** Full-width yellow background card with arrow CTA button (dark pill) and ✕ close — high visibility interrupt
- **Asset widget (BNB):** White card with coin icon, price, green percentage change with sparkline chart — compact market snapshot
- **P2P widget:** Paired card with token pair, action label ("Buy" in green), and current price
- **Fear & Greed widget:** Card with gauge/meter visualization — partially visible, teasing scroll
- **Secondary horizontal tab bar:** Text tabs with active underline indicator, notification dot on "Following"

---

## Typography & Color

**Hierarchy:** Portfolio number is the dominant typographic element — large, bold, near-monospace weight. Section labels (Earn, P2P) are small, muted gray. Asset prices are medium-bold. Supporting data (fiat equivalent, PNL) is small and subdued.

**Color palette:**
- Background: Near-white (#F5F5F5 system gray)
- Accent/Primary: Golden yellow — used for CTAs, badges, banner background, subscribe button
- Positive: Green (price change, "Buy" label, sparkline)
- Negative: Red (PNL value)
- Cards: Pure white with subtle shadow
- Text: Near-black primary, medium gray secondary

Color is used functionally: yellow = action/attention, green = positive/buy, red = loss/negative. No decorative color use.

---

## Patterns

- **Value concealment toggle:** The chevron (∧) next to "Est. Total Value (BTC)" implies hide/show balance — privacy-first pattern embedded inline rather than as a separate control
- **PNL expandable row:** Today's PNL has its own chevron, suggesting drill-down into performance detail without leaving the screen — progressive disclosure of financial data
- **Modular widget grid as personalization surface:** The "Discover personalized home experiences" banner directly references the widget cards below — the screen is teaching users that these cards are configurable, connecting UI affordance to onboarding
- **Asymmetric card pairing:** BNB card (left) has sparkline; P2P card (right) has different data model entirely — the grid doesn't enforce visual uniformity, allowing each widget to optimize for its content type
- **Dual tab systems:** Secondary horizontal tabs (Discover/Following/Campaign…) sit above the main bottom nav, creating a content-layer tab system distinct from navigation tabs — two tab bars coexisting without confusion
- **Sparkline as trust signal:** Tiny inline chart on asset card communicates trend without numbers — emotional data, not analytical
- **Search bar as announcement surface:** The search field contains a hashtag promotional string — repurposing a utility component as a low-friction marketing channel

---

## Notes
- Yellow as single accent color does heavy lifting — CTA + badge + banner + promo all unified
- PNL row = great pattern for surfacing contextual data without dedicated screen
- Widget grid = modular dashboard pattern; each card is self-contained unit
- Two-tab-bar pattern worth studying — content tabs vs nav tabs, visually differentiated by position + weight
- "Add Funds" floated beside balance = right-place-right-time CTA placement
- Dismissible banner with arrow button inside = more intentional than a plain text link
- Fear & Greed meter widget = gamified/emotional data visualization in a financial context — humanizes the data

## Screen
Fitness/health tracking app — daily activity summary screen. Sits within a date-browsable history flow, accessible after selecting a specific day. Layout flows vertically: nav header → week strip → hero ring visualization → metric detail with mini chart → secondary stats grid → tertiary stats.

---

## Layout & Spacing

Content is organized in a clear visual hierarchy with generous breathing room between sections. The ring takes up roughly 40% of the screen height — intentionally dominant. Below it, stats are left-aligned with consistent margins. Steps and Distance use a 2-column grid. A subtle horizontal rule separates the chart section from the secondary stats. The week strip sits tight to the header, functioning as both navigation and context.

---

## Components

- **Week strip**: 7 small ring icons (mini versions of the hero ring), days labeled above, current day highlighted with a white circle badge. Incomplete rings shown as dark outlines.
- **Hero ring**: Large donut/arc progress indicator. Filled portion in vivid red-pink, unfilled in deep maroon. Arrow icon embedded at the progress endpoint — clever affordance suggesting tappability.
- **Mini bar chart**: Sparse vertical bars in red on a black field, dotted baseline, time labels on x-axis, calorie label on y-axis. Minimal gridlines — only one dotted reference line.
- **Metric label + value**: Label in small white, value in large bold red-pink with unit in smaller caps inline.
- **Stats grid**: Two-column layout, label above value, white text, no card backgrounds — flat on black.
- **Bottom tab bar**: Pill-shaped dark container, active state uses chartreuse/green ring icon + colored label.

---

## Typography & Color

**Type hierarchy:**
- Date header: Medium weight, white, truncated
- Metric category label ("Move"): Small, white, regular weight
- Primary value (46/250CAL): Large, bold, red-pink — the most visually prominent text
- Secondary stats (Steps, Distance): Medium-large, white, bold values
- Chart labels: Tiny, muted white/gray

**Color palette:**
- Background: Pure black `#000`
- Primary accent: Vivid red-pink (coral-red) — used for active ring fill, primary values, chart bars
- Dark maroon: Ring track/unfilled state — same hue, heavily darkened
- White: All labels and secondary text
- Chartreuse/green: Active tab indicator only — creates strong contrast against the red system

Color is used functionally: red = move/calorie metric; green = active navigation state. The maroon track communicates "remaining" without introducing a new color.

---

## Patterns

- **Miniaturized hero as navigation**: The week strip uses tiny versions of the main ring as day selectors — the visualization language is consistent at every scale, reinforcing the mental model.
- **Progress endpoint as CTA**: Placing an arrow icon at the tip of the progress arc is a non-obvious pattern — it makes the ring feel interactive and directional rather than purely decorative.
- **Mixed-weight inline units**: "46/250**CAL**" — the unit is set in smaller caps inline with the number, keeping the value scannable without breaking to a new line or using a separate label.
- **Sparse chart as ambient data**: The bar chart isn't meant to be read precisely — it's an activity texture showing when in the day movement happened. Intentionally low-information density.
- **Dark maroon as "empty" state**: Using a darkened version of the same hue for the unfilled ring track (rather than gray) keeps the palette monochromatic and avoids visual noise.
- **Chartreuse as single-accent contrast**: One tab uses a completely different hue (green) against an otherwise red/white system — makes the active state unmissable without competing with content.

---

## Notes

- Mini ring strip = brilliant nav pattern for any streak/history UI
- Arrow at arc tip = turns static progress into perceived affordance
- Maroon-as-empty: always use darkened same-hue for track, not neutral gray
- Sparse bar chart = "activity texture" not data table — design for feel not precision
- Black bg + single vivid accent = maximum ring legibility
- Chartreuse tab pop: reserve a contrasting hue for ONE nav state only
- Inline unit in small caps keeps number dominant without extra label
- Consider: hero viz takes 40% height — commit to it or lose it

## Screen
Audio meditation or mindfulness player screen. Full-screen now-playing view within a wellness/meditation app. This sits deep in the content flow — after a user has selected and started a guided session. Layout is three-zone: metadata header, immersive visual center, playback controls footer.

## Layout & Spacing
Full bleed dark background with no visible padding constraints — the visual field owns the entire screen. Header metadata sits top-left with generous breathing room from the edges. Controls are pinned to the bottom with comfortable vertical spacing from the progress bar. The center zone is intentionally empty of UI — reserved entirely for the ambient visual. This negative space is a deliberate design choice, not an oversight.

## Components
- **Session metadata block** — title in white, creator name and duration in muted gray, stacked left-aligned. No avatar, no artwork thumbnail — pure text.
- **Ambient orb / glow visual** — a soft radial gradient bloom (yellow-green core fading to dark brown/black). Functions as both artwork and mood-setter. Likely animated during playback.
- **Progress bar** — ultra-thin single line, low contrast, nearly invisible. Timestamp only on the right (elapsed). No total duration shown on bar.
- **Playback controls row** — five elements: cast/airplay icon, rewind 10s, pause (circle-outlined, larger), forward 10s, heart/favorite. Pause button uses outline circle style rather than filled — keeps it light.
- **Close (×) button** — top right, dismisses the player.
- **Overflow (···)** — secondary options, bottom-right of header area.

## Typography & Color
Tight two-level hierarchy: session title in white (~18–20pt, medium weight), subtitle metadata in warm gray (~14pt, regular). No decorative type. Color palette is extremely restrained — near-black background (#1a1200 range), warm olive-brown midtones, yellow-green accent glow. Controls and text are white/light gray. Color is used atmospherically, not informationally — the glow is mood, not data.

## Patterns
- **Ambient visualization as artwork** — instead of album art or illustration, the "artwork" is a living, breathing light orb. This ties the visual directly to the audio experience rather than representing it statically.
- **Intentional UI suppression** — the screen deliberately minimizes chrome. No waveform, no chapter markers, no speed control visible. Reduces cognitive load to match the meditative intent.
- **Progress bar as whisper** — the seek bar is so thin and low-contrast it barely registers. This discourages scrubbing/time-anxiety, which would break the meditative state. Brilliant alignment of UX behavior with product purpose.
- **Elapsed-only timestamp** — showing 0:04 elapsed rather than a countdown or full duration removes the "how much longer" anxiety loop.
- **Outline-only play/pause** — the circle outline (not filled pill or solid button) keeps the control present but visually recessive, letting the ambient visual dominate.
- **No album art paradigm** — completely abandons the standard music player visual convention. The glow IS the experience, not a representation of it.

## Notes
- Glow orb = content, not decoration. Could animate: pulse, color shift, expand on inhale cues
- Progress bar intentionally invisible — fight the urge to make it prominent in calm/focus apps
- Elapsed-only timer = anti-anxiety pattern. Steal for sleep/focus timers
- Outline circle button = softer than filled, less "action-y" — good for passive playback states
- Full-bleed dark + single warm accent color = extremely achievable but feels premium
- No artwork = brave. Works when the visual IS the mood
- Consider: does the orb color change per session/instructor/mood category?
- Metadata left-aligned, controls centered — classic asymmetry that still feels balanced

## Screen
Event creation form in a social/events app. This screen sits mid-flow after the user has already selected a date/time and location — it's the detail configuration step before publishing. Layout is a single scrollable column with a fixed CTA at the bottom. Primary interactive elements: address block, map preview, description field, toggle, price selector, visibility and capacity pickers, and the create button.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Content is organized into implicit sections — location info, map, description, then two labeled groups ("Ticketing" and "Options") — without heavy dividers. Grouped rows sit inside rounded-rect cards with consistent internal padding. Generous vertical breathing room between section groups creates rhythm without using hard lines. The map breaks the form cadence intentionally, acting as a visual anchor mid-scroll.

---

## Components
- **Location card**: Teal-tinted filled card, two-line address + secondary door code row below with a subtle separator. Icon-left alignment throughout.
- **Map embed**: Full-bleed within its rounded container, no border, standard pin marker. Feels ambient rather than interactive.
- **Description field**: Ghost/outline style, icon-left, placeholder text only — minimal visual weight.
- **Toggle (Require Approval)**: Standard iOS-style toggle, off state. Right-aligned within its row.
- **Disclosure rows (Price, Visibility, Capacity)**: Label left, current value + chevron/stepper right. Clean two-column row pattern.
- **CTA button**: Full-width pill, muted gray — visually indicates incomplete/not-yet-active state rather than a vibrant color.

---

## Typography & Color
- **Type hierarchy**: Nav title is medium weight, centered. Section labels ("Ticketing," "Options") are small, muted, uppercase-adjacent — subdued section headers. Row labels are regular weight; current values are slightly bolder or same weight but right-aligned for contrast.
- **Color palette**: Muted teal/slate as the dominant background and card color. White or near-white for text on dark surfaces. The map introduces natural greens and beige as a visual break. The CTA button is a desaturated gray, signaling passive/incomplete state.
- **Functional color use**: Teal unifies the location block and overall chrome. Red pin on map is the only warm accent, drawing the eye to the confirmed location.

---

## Patterns

**Contextual map as form feedback** — embedding a live map directly in the creation flow confirms the address visually without requiring the user to leave the screen. It's passive validation that doubles as spatial reassurance.

**Muted CTA as form completion signal** — the "Create Event" button is intentionally desaturated/gray rather than a primary action color. This subtly communicates that required fields may still be incomplete, without using error states or disabling the button outright.

**Icon-as-semantic-label** — every row uses a small icon instead of or alongside a text label (lock for approval, dollar for price, globe for visibility). This compresses the UI while adding scannable meaning.

**Grouped settings with plain-text section headers** — "Ticketing" and "Options" act as lightweight section breaks without adding visual noise. Feels native and familiar.

**Profile avatar + dropdown in nav** — placing a user avatar with a caret in the top-left suggests account-switching or context selection (perhaps creating the event as a different persona/host), which is a non-standard but smart pattern for multi-identity apps.

**Door code as secondary location detail** — surfacing access info (door code) directly in the location block is a thoughtful domain-specific decision, reducing the need for a separate field or post-creation message.

---

## Notes
- Muted CTA = passive completion signal, no need to disable
- Map embed mid-form = spatial confirmation without navigation
- Icon-left rows compress label space, improve scannability
- Teal monochrome palette keeps form calm, low cognitive load
- Avatar+caret in nav = host/context switcher — worth stealing
- Section headers as plain small text > heavy dividers
- Door code inline with address = smart domain detail surfacing
- Consider: map tap → expand/edit location?

## Screen
Fitness tracking app home screen for a new or inactive user. This is the empty/onboarding state of the dashboard — no recorded activities yet. Scrollable feed layout with distinct content sections stacked vertically. Primary goal is to drive first activity recording and goal-setting engagement.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Three distinct card/section zones separated by subtle dividers or background breaks: (1) Suggested Goal carousel at top, (2) personalized CTA block, (3) Suggested Challenges feed. Each section has generous internal padding (~20px+). The goal card uses a horizontal row layout with icon + text left, action button right. Sections feel breathable without wasted space. Dividers between sections use full-bleed white-on-light-gray contrast rather than lines.

---

## Components
- **Goal Card (carousel):** White card with circular icon, bold metric text, progress subtext, and a filled orange pill CTA button. Pagination dots below indicate swipeable content.
- **"Customize" link:** Orange text-only link in the section header — low visual weight secondary action.
- **Primary CTA Button (full-width):** Large, rounded-rectangle, solid orange — dominates its section. Highest visual weight on screen.
- **Challenge Card:** Rounded white card with social proof line at top, illustrated character art left, bold title + description right, and a small yellow reward badge ("Digital Trophy") at bottom. Partially reveals a second card to signal horizontal scroll.
- **Nav Bar:** Icon + label tabs; active state uses orange fill icon; Record tab has a distinct filled circle icon with a notification dot on Maps.

---

## Typography & Color
- **Type hierarchy:** Section labels in medium-weight all-caps-adjacent style; card titles in bold large (~22–24px); body copy in regular weight, smaller (~14–15px); metadata/progress text in light gray.
- **Personalization:** First name used in headline ("Ready to get moving, Alex?") — conversational, direct.
- **Color palette:** White backgrounds, light gray page background, black text, orange (#E8490F-ish) as the single accent color used for CTAs, active nav, and links. Yellow used sparingly for the reward badge. Color is strictly functional — orange = action/active, yellow = reward/achievement.

---

## Patterns
- **Empty state as opportunity:** Rather than showing blank stats, the screen fills the void with goal suggestions and challenges — zero-data state becomes an onboarding funnel without feeling like onboarding.
- **Social proof on challenge card:** "More than 1,066,000 athletes have already joined" sits above the challenge content — FOMO/credibility before the ask.
